<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/sense/lobsters/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: ### Lobsters Rails Project [![build status](https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/actions/workflows/check.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/actions/workflows/check.yml)
2: 
3: [Lobsters](https://lobste.rs) is a Rails codebase and uses a SQL (MariaDB in production) backend for the database.
4: The code is open source as part of our [commitment to transparency](https://lobste.rs/about#transparency).
5: It's been used to run [sister sites](https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/blob/main/sister_sites.md), but mostly we want people to be able to understand and improve what's happening on Lobsters itself.
6: 
7: Despite the site being an [uber mega cringe](https://bsky.app/profile/anirudh.fi/post/3mdikk6u2w22e) [ghost town](https://xcancel.com/webshitweekly/status/1399935275057389571) running on a [quite sad codebase](https://web.archive.org/web/20230213161624/https://old.reddit.com/r/rails/comments/6jz7tq/source_code_lobsters_a_hacker_news_clone_built/), at least we have [no relation](https://lobste.rs/about#michaelbolton) to the self-help guru.
8: 
9: 
10: #### Contributing bugfixes and new features
11: 
12: We'd love to have your help.
13: Please see the [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) file for details and dev environment setup.
14: If you have questions, there is usually someone in [our chat room](https://lobste.rs/chat) who's familiar with the code.
15: 
16: Lobsters is a volunteer project with limited development time and a long time horizon, we hope to be running for decades.
17: So our design philosophy is a little different than a typical commercial product:
18: 
19:  * We started with Rails 3.2.2 in 2012, so we have a few dusty corners and places where we don't take advantage of features that were introduced since we started.
20:  * We lean into using Rails features instead of custom code, and we'll write a couple dozen lines of narrow code for a feature rather than add a dependency that might require maintenance.
21:  * We are very reluctant to add new production services and almost entirely unwilling to depend on external services.
22:    We take pride in self-hosting from the VPS up, which necessitates reducing the number of moving parts.
23:  * We test to ensure functionality, but testing is a lot lighter for moderator and other non-core features.
24:    We're trying to maximize the return on investment of testing rather than minimize errors.
25:  * We're willing to take downtime for big code changes rather than try to make them seamless.
26:  * We have users that are unusually likely to use old, experimental, and even homemade browsers, so we only use CSS in the [widely available baseline](https://web-platform-dx.github.io/web-features/).
27:  * We don't serve JavaScript to logged-out visitors, we use as little JS as possible for users with a vanilla/jQuery-style of DOM attachment, we are (very slowly) making JS entirely optional for users, and we accept JS for mod features.
28:    Keeping JS small and optional helps keep the site fast, and an unusually high proportion of visitors use old, odd, and homebrew browsers.
29:    We do not want JavaScript in our build chain, it requires too much maintenance.
30: 
31: Please see [CONTRIBUTING.md](/CONTRIBUTING.md) for setup.
32: 
33: ## Production
34: 
35: You are free to use this code to start your own [sister site](/sister_sites.md)
36: because the code is available under a [permissive license](https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/blob/main/LICENSE) (3-clause BSD).
37: We welcome bug reports and code contributions that help use improve [lobste.rs](https://lobste.rs).
38: As a volunteer project we're reluctant to take on work that's not useful to our site, so please understand if we don't want to adopt your custom feature.
39: These instructions assume you know the basics of web development with Ruby on Rails on a Linux server.
40: 
41: Important: the hard part about starting an online community is not the codebase.
42: A new social site has to solve a chicken-and-egg problem:
43: nobody will want to participate on a new site until other people are participating.
44: Before you start working with the code, make a plan for how you'll reach potential community members and what they'll find engaging about the early days.
45: If you don't attract enough early users to reach a self-sustaining level of activity, the code doesn't matter!
46: 
47: As of February 2025 we have a [Zulip-based chat room](https://lobsters.zulipchat.com) to discuss the codebase and offer limited support to owners of sister sites like warnings about breaking changes and vulnerability announcements.
48: If you run a site using the codebase, you will benefit from joining.
49: 
50: Setup:
51: 
52: 1. Fork the repo, clone it to your local computer.
53:    You should add lobsters as a git remote so you can continue to pull our changes.
54: 
55: 2. Edit `config/application.rb` to put in your site's name and domain name.
56: 
57: 3. We use a paid service called [Hatchbox](https://hatchbox.io) to set up and deploy the server.
58:    Reusing this config will be much easier than Heroku/Render/etc.
59: ...Hatchbox has a clever wizard-style flow for getting started.
60:    I'm going to explain what our final settings are rather than try to stay current with the wizard setup.
61:    This should be all the info you need, just in a slightly different order.
62: 
63:   * Follow the [Hatchbox Docs](https://hatchbox.relationkit.io/) to create an account and connect a Hosting Provider.
64:     We use DigitalOcean because I was already familiar with it.
65:   * Create a Cluster, ours is called `lobsters`.
66:     We don't have any Cluster settings customized.
67:   * In your Cluster, create a Server.
68:     There's a [DO limitation](https://www.digitalocean.com/community/questions/how-do-i-create-a-reverse-dns-ptr-record) that the server name must match your domain name for them to create a reverse DNS PTR record that you'll need for email.
69:     We don't have any Server settings customized.
70:   * When a server is created, check if its [IP address is blacklisted for sending email](https://dnschecker.org/ip-blacklist-checker.php?query=68.183.100.95).
71:     Email spammers constantly try to create servers on every hosting provider.
72:     The providers ban them, but the IP address gets a bad reputation and may be on blocklists when it's assigned to you.
73:     Check your server's IP ASAP; it's much easier to delete and recreate than get off the blocklists, especially because outsiders don't have any insight into the internal blocklists of big email providers like Google, Apple, and Microsoft.
74:   * You'll need to create a Database.
75:     We use MariaDB in prod but are working to [migrate to SQLite](https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/issues/539).
76:   * Create an App. Running through the Settings sections:
77:     * Processes: Add a `solid_queue` process, command `bundle exec rails solid_queue:start`.
78:     * Activity: This is logs, nothing to change.
79:     * Repository: Connect your GitHub repo.
80:     * Domains & SSL: Add your domain names, include both `example.org` and `www.example.org`.
81:     * Environment:
82: 
83:       ```
84:       BUNDLE_WITHOUT      development:test
85:       DATABASE_URL        trilogy://[username]:[password]@[1.2.3.4]/lobsters
86:       INGRESS_PASSWORD    [random generated key]
87:       PORT                9000
88:       RACK_ENV            production
89:       RAILS_ENV           production
90:       RAILS_LOG_TO_STDOUT true
91:       RAILS_MAX_THREADS   10
92:       SECRET_KEY_BASE     [random generated key]
93:       ```
94: 
95:       Search the codebase for uses of the `ENV` global for more that can be easily configured.
96: 
97:     * Databases: We manage this independently of Hatchbox for historic reasons, see `#539`.
98:     * Cron Jobs:
99: 
100:       ```
101:       expire_page_cache         * * * * *      script/expire_page_cache
102:       script/lobsters-cron    */5 * * * *      bundle exec script/lobsters-cron
103:       ```
104: 
105:     * Settings:
106:       We have tweaks of production config files and we want those [tracked in our git repo](https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/tree/main/hatchbox).
107:       We have rigged up settings to run an (unfortunately) clever hook to update those on deploy, see below.
108: 
109:       Pre-build script: `hatchbox/pre-build`
110:       Custom build script: blank
111:       Post-build script: blank
112:       Post-deploy script: `hatchbox/post-deploy`
113:       Failed deploy script: blank
114:       Caddyfile: copy the text of the file `hatchbox/Caddyfile` from this repo.
115:         As it says, you have to manually paste it in on changes and click 'Update Caddy'.
116: 
117:   * Make a deployment with Hatchbox. Whew!
118: 
119: 4. SSH into your server as the `root` user to set up the deploy hook.
120: 
121:     ```
122:     ln -s /home/deploy/lobsters/current/hatchbox/root-deploy.service /etc/systemd/system
123:     ln -s /home/deploy/lobsters/current/hatchbox/root-deploy.path /etc/systemd/system
124:     systemctl daemon-reload
125:     systemctl enable --now root-deploy.service # first backup takes 10ish min
126:     systemctl enable --now root-deploy.path
127:     ```
128: 
129: 5. Deploy again with Hatchbox to run the hook and finish the server provisioning.
130: 
131: 6. SSH into your server as the `deploy` user
132: 
133:    1. Run `rails credentials:edit` to set up credentials there, like you did for development.
134:       Use `config/credentials.yml.sample` for a template.
135:       On setup, Rails will give you new random value for `secret_key_base` and you can use `rails secret` any time you need to generate another.
136:       Never `git commit` or share your `config/credentials.yml.enc` or `config/master.key`.
137:    2. [Test your mail config for spamminess](https://www.mail-tester.com/).
138:       Run `echo "Test Postfix email, visit scnenic https://example.com" | mail -s "Postfix Test" test-whatever@srv1.mailtester.com`
139:    3. Run `rails console` and create a `User` for yourself, set `is_admin = true`.
140:       You'll probably also have to create a `Category` and one `Tag` for the site to run at all.
141:    4. See logs in `~deploy/sitename/shared/log`.
142: 
143: If everything worked, you should have a running instance now.
144: 
145: 
146: #### Administration
147: 
148: Basic moderation happens on-site, but some administrative tasks require use of the rails console in production.
149: Administrators can create and edit tags at `/tags`, the mod dashboard is at `/mod`.

(End of file - total 149 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/sense/lobsters/app/models/story.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # typed: false
2: 
3: class Story < ApplicationRecord
4:   belongs_to :user
5:   belongs_to :domain, optional: true, counter_cache: true
6:   belongs_to :origin, optional: true, counter_cache: true
7:   belongs_to :merged_into_story,
8:     class_name: "Story",
9:     counter_cache: :stories_count,
10:     foreign_key: "merged_story_id",
11:     inverse_of: :merged_stories,
12:     optional: true
13:   has_many :merged_stories,
14:     class_name: "Story",
15:     foreign_key: "merged_story_id",
16:     inverse_of: :merged_into_story,
17:     dependent: :nullify
18:   has_many :taggings,
19:     autosave: true,
20:     dependent: :destroy # tests fail if :restrict_with_exception
21:   has_many :suggested_taggings, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
22:   has_many :suggested_tags, source: :story, through: :suggested_taggings, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
23:   has_many :suggested_titles, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
24:   has_many :suggested_tagging_times,
25:     -> { group(:tag_id).select("count(*) as times, tag_id").order(times: :desc) },
26:     class_name: "SuggestedTagging",
27:     inverse_of: :story
28:   has_many :suggested_title_times,
29:     -> { group(:title).select("count(*) as times, title").order(times: :desc) },
30:     class_name: "SuggestedTitle",
31:     inverse_of: :story
32:   has_many :comments,
33:     inverse_of: :story,
34:     dependent: :restrict_with_exception
35:   has_many :tags, -> { order("tags.is_media desc, tags.tag") }, through: :taggings
36:   has_many :votes, -> { where(comment_id: nil) },
37:     inverse_of: :story,
38:     dependent: :destroy # tests fail if :restrict_with_exception
39:   has_many :voters, -> { where("votes.comment_id" => nil) },
40:     through: :votes,
41:     source: :user
42:   has_many :hidings, class_name: "HiddenStory", inverse_of: :story, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
43:   has_many :savings, class_name: "SavedStory", inverse_of: :story, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
44:   has_one :story_text, foreign_key: :id, dependent: :restrict_with_exception, inverse_of: :story
45:   has_many :links,
46:     inverse_of: :from_story,
47:     dependent: :destroy # tests fail if :restrict_with_exception
48:   has_many :incoming_links,
49:     class_name: "Link",
50:     inverse_of: :to_story,
51:     dependent: :restrict_with_exception
52:   has_one :moderation, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
53:   has_many :read_ribbons, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
54:   has_many :saved_stories, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
55: 
56:   validates :short_id,
57:     presence: true,
58:     uniqueness: {case_sensitive: false},
59:     length: {maximum: 6}
60: 
61:   scope :base, ->(user, unmerged: true) {
62:     q = includes(:hidings, :story_text, :user).not_deleted(user).mod_preload?(user)
63:     q = q.unmerged if unmerged
64:     q
65:   }
66:   scope :for_presentation, -> {
67:     includes(:domain, :origin, :hidings, :user, :tags, taggings: :tag)
68:   }
69:   scope :mod_preload?, ->(user) {
70:     user.try(:is_moderator?) ? preload(:suggested_taggings, :suggested_titles) : all
71:   }
72:   scope :mod_single_preload?, ->(user) {
73:     user.try(:is_moderator?) ? preload(:suggested_taggings, :suggested_titles, hidings: :user, votes: :user) : all
74:   }
75:   scope :deleted, -> { where(is_deleted: true) }
76:   scope :not_deleted, ->(user) {
77:     user.try(:is_moderator?) ? all : where(is_deleted: false).or(where(user_id: user.try(:id).to_i))
78:   }
79:   scope :unmerged, -> { where(merged_story_id: nil) }
80:   scope :positive_ranked, -> { where("score >= 0") }
81:   scope :low_scoring, ->(max = 5) { where("score < ?", max) }
82:   scope :front_page, -> { hottest.limit(StoriesPaginator::STORIES_PER_PAGE) }
83:   scope :hottest, ->(user = nil, exclude_tags = nil) {
84:     base(user).not_hidden_by(user)
85:       .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
86:       .positive_ranked
87:       .order(:hotness)
88:   }
89: 
90:   scope :tagged, ->(user, tags) {
91:     base(user)
92:       .positive_ranked
93:       .where(
94:         Tagging
95:           .where("story_id = stories.id")
96:           .where(tag_id: tags)
97:           .arel.exists
98:       )
99:       .order(created_at: :desc)
100:   }
101: 
102:   scope :top, ->(user, length, exclude_tags = nil) {
103:     raise ArgumentError, "Invalid interval" unless IntervalHelper::TIME_INTERVALS.value?(length[:intv].capitalize)
104: 
105:     top = base(user)
106:       .where("created_at >= (NOW() - INTERVAL ? #{length[:intv].upcase})", length[:dur])
107:       .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
108:     top.order(score: :desc)
109:   }
110: 
111:   scope :categories, ->(user, categories) {
112:     base(user)
113:       .positive_ranked
114:       .where(
115:         Tagging
116:           .joins(:tag)
117:           .where("story_id = stories.id")
118:           .where(tag: {category_id: categories})
119:           .arel.exists
120:       )
121:       .order(created_at: :desc)
122:   }
123: 
124:   scope :recent, ->(user = nil, exclude_tags = nil, unmerged: true) {
125:     base(user, unmerged: unmerged).not_hidden_by(user)
126:       .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
127:       .low_scoring
128:       .where("created_at >= ?", 10.days.ago)
129:       .where.not(id: front_page.ids)
130:       .order("stories.created_at DESC")
131:   }
132:   scope :filter_tags, ->(tags) {
133:     tags.empty? ? all : where(
134:       Story.arel_table[:id].not_in(
135:         Tagging.where(tag_id: tags).select(:story_id).arel
136:       )
137:     )
138:   }
139:   scope :filter_tags_for, ->(user) {
140:     user.nil? ? all : where(
141:       Story.arel_table[:id].not_in(
142:         Tagging.joins(tag: :tag_filters)
143:           .where(tag_filters: {user_id: user})
144:           .select(:story_id).arel
145:       )
146:     )
147:   }
148:   scope :hidden_by, ->(user) {
149:     user.nil? ? none : joins(:hidings).merge(HiddenStory.by(user))
150:   }
151:   scope :not_hidden_by, ->(user) {
152:     user.nil? ? all : where.not(
153:       HiddenStory.select("TRUE")
154:         .where(Arel.sql("hidden_stories.story_id = stories.id"))
155:         .by(user)
156:         .arel
157:         .exists
158:     )
159:   }
160:   scope :saved_by, ->(user) {
161:     user.nil? ? none : joins(:savings).merge(SavedStory.by(user))
162:   }
163:   scope :to_mastodon, -> {
164:     hottest(nil, Tag.where(tag: "meta").ids)
165:       .where(mastodon_id: nil)
166:       .where("score >= 2")
167:       .where("created_at >= ?", 2.days.ago)
168:       .limit(10)
169:   }
170: 
171:   scope :hidden, ->(user, exclude_tags = nil) {
172:     base(user)
173:       .hidden_by(user)
174:       .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
175:       .order(:hotness)
176:   }
177: 
178:   scope :newest, ->(user, exclude_tags = nil) {
179:     base(user, unmerged: false)
180:       .filter_tags(exclude_tags || [])
181:       .order(id: :desc)
182:   }
183: 
184:   scope :active, ->(user, exclude_tags = []) {
185:     base(user)
186:       .where.not(id: hidden_by(user).select(:id))
187:       .filter_tags(exclude_tags)
188:       .order(last_comment_at: :desc)
189:   }
190: 
191:   scope :saved, ->(user, exclude_tags = []) {
192:     base(user)
193:       .saved_by(user)
194:       .filter_tags(exclude_tags)
195:       .order(:hotness)
196:   }
197: 
198:   scope :newest_by_user, ->(user, submitter) {
199:     where(user: submitter)
200:       .includes(:tags)
201:       .not_deleted(user)
202:       .mod_preload?(user)
203:       .order(id: :desc)
204:   }
205: 
206:   include Token
207: 
208:   TITLE_MIN_LENGTH = 3
209:   TITLE_MAX_LENGTH = 150
210: 
211:   validates :title, length: {in: TITLE_MIN_LENGTH..TITLE_MAX_LENGTH}, presence: true
212:   validates :description, length: {maximum: 65_535}
213:   validates :url, length: {maximum: 250, allow_nil: true}
214:   validates :short_id, presence: true, length: {maximum: 6}
215:   validates :markeddown_description, length: {maximum: 16_777_215, allow_nil: true}
216:   validates :mastodon_id, length: {maximum: 25, allow_nil: true}

242:   # users needed to make similar suggestions go live

245:   # let a hot story linger for this many seconds

248:   # drop these words from titles when making URLs

286:       errors.add(:title, " doesn't need to scream, ASCII has supported lowercase since June 17, 1963.")

336:   def check_not_new_domain_from_new_user
337:     return unless url.present? && new_record? && domain
338: 
339:     if user&.is_new? && domain.stories.not_deleted(nil).count == 0
340:       errors.add :url, "is an unseen domain from a new user."
341:       self.new_user_acculturation_error = ModMail.create_inviter_discussion! user: user, message: <<~MESSAGE
342:         Hi,
343: 
344:         #{user.username}: This has a high false-positive rate, but we don't allow new users to submit links to domains we haven't seen before.
345:         This is to give you a time to learn about topicality and to discourage immediate self-promotion.
346: 
347:         #{user.invited_by_user&.username}: This is a good opportunity for you to introduce #{user.username} to the [site guideline](https://lobste.rs/about#guidelines) around topicality and self-promo.
348: 
349:         If you need, you can talk to the mods in this conversation, but we'll probably stay out of it unless asked.
350: 
351:         #{submission_markdown}

353:     end

374:   def check_not_brigading
375:     return if url.blank? || !new_record? || !(
376:       url.match?(%r{^https?://bitbucket.org/[^/]+/[^/]+/(issues|pull-requests)/}) ||
377:       url.match?(%r{^https?://bugs.launchpad.net/[^/]+/\+bug/}) ||
378:       url.match?(%r{^https?://chiselapp.com/user/[^/]+/repository/[^/]+/tktview/}) ||
379:       url.match?(%r{^https?://codeberg.org/[^/]+/[^/]+/(issues|pulls)/}) ||
380:       url.match?(%r{^https?://github.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/(discussions|issues|pull)/}) ||
381:       url.match?(%r{^https?://gitlab.com/.+/(issues|merge_requests)/}) ||
382:       url.match?(%r{^https?://savannah.gnu.org/bugs/}) ||
383:       url.match?(%r{^https?://sourceforge.net/p/[^/]+/(support|tickets)/})
384:     )
385: 
386:     if user.is_new?
387:       errors.add :url, "is a project's bug tracker or discussions"
388:       self.new_user_acculturation_error = ModMail.create_inviter_discussion! user: user, message: <<~MESSAGE
389:         Hi,
390: 
391:         #{user.username}: We don't accept links into projects' bug trackers or discussions to avoid brigading our readers into their community spaces,
392:         and because Lobsters doesn't have good threads when we're dropped without context into the middle of a controversy.
393: 
394:         #{user.invited_by_user&.username}: This is a good opportunity for you to introduce #{user.username} to the [brigading guideline](https://lobste.rs/about#brigading) and maybe help them find a better link, like an overview from a neutral third party.
395: 
396:         If you need, you can talk to the mods in this conversation, but we'll probably stay out of it unless asked.
397: 
398:         #{submission_markdown}
399:       MESSAGE
400:     else
401:       errors.add :url, <<~EXPLANATION
402:         is to a project's bug tracker or discussions; see the Guidelines on brigading. It's bad for
403:         projects when we dump 100k+ people into their community spaces, and Lobsters doesn't have good
404:         threads when we're dropped without context into the middle of a controversy. If you weren't
405:         trying to brigade the site into a fight you are involved in: find an overview, preferably from
406:         a neutral third party. If you were trying to do that: don't.
407:       EXPLANATION
408:       ModNote.tattle_on_brigading!(self)
409:     end
410:   end

461:     return Story.none if url.blank?

463:     @_similar_stories ||= Story.find_similar_by_url(url).order(id: :desc)
464:     # do not include this story itself or any story merged into it

467:         .where("merged_story_id is null or merged_story_id != ?", id)
468:     end
469:     # do not include the story this one is merged into
470:     if merged_story_id?
471:       @_similar_stories = @_similar_stories.where.not(id: merged_story_id)
472:     end
473:     @_similar_stories

504:   def as_json(options = {})
505:     keys = [
506:       :short_id,
507:       :created_at,
508:       :title,
509:       :url,
510:       :score,
511:       :score,
512:       :flags,
513:       {comment_count: :comments_count},
514:       {description: :markeddown_description},
515:       {description_plain: :description},
516:       {submitter_user: user.username},
517:       :user_is_author,
518:       {tags: tags.map(&:tag).sort}
519:     ]
520:     if options && options[:with_comments]
521:       keys.push(comments: options[:with_comments])
522:     end
523: 
524:     json = {}
525:     keys.each do |k|
526:       if k.is_a?(Symbol)
527:         json[k] = send(k)
528:       elsif k.is_a?(Hash)
529:         json[k.keys.first] = if k.values.first.is_a?(Symbol)
530:           send(k.values.first)
531:         else
532:           k.values.first
533:         end
534:       end
535:     end
536: 
537:     json[:short_id_url] = Routes.story_short_id_url self
538:     json[:comments_url] = Routes.title_url self
539: 
540:     json
541:   end

543:   def assign_initial_attributes
544:     self.short_id = ShortId.new(self.class).generate
545:     self.score ||= 1 # tests are allowed to fake out the score
546:     self.hotness = calculated_hotness
547:     self.last_edited_at = Time.current
548:   end
549: 
550:   def calculated_hotness
551:     # take each tag's hotness modifier into effect, and give a slight bump to
552:     # stories submitted by the author
553:     base = tags.sum(:hotness_mod) + ((user_is_author? && url.present?) ? 0.25 : 0.0)
554: 
555:     # give a story's comment votes some weight, ignoring submitter's comments
556:     cpoints = if base < 0
557:       0
558:     else
559:       merged_comments.where.not(user_id: user_id).sum("comments.score + 1").to_f * 0.5
560:     end
561: 
562:     # mix in any stories this one cannibalized
563:     cpoints += merged_stories.map(&:score).inject(&:+).to_f
564: 
565:     # if a story has many comments but few votes, it's probably a bad story, so
566:     # cap the comment points at the number of upvotes
567:     cpoints = [self.score, cpoints].min
568: 
569:     # don't immediately kill stories at 0 by bumping up score by one
570:     order = Math.log([(score + 1).abs + cpoints, 1].max, 10)
571:     sign = if score > 0
572:       1
573:     elsif score < 0
574:       -1
575:     else
576:       0
577:     end
578: 
579:     -((order * sign) + base + ((created_at || Time.current).to_f / HOTNESS_WINDOW)).round(7)
580:   end
581: 
582:   def can_be_seen_by_user?(user)
583:     !is_gone? || user&.is_moderator? || user&.id == user_id
584:   end

586:   def can_have_images?
587:     # doesn't test self.editor so a user can't trick a mod into editing a
588:     # story to enable an image
589:     return false if user.nil?
590: 
591:     return user.is_moderator? if previewing
592: 
593:     current_time = Time.current
594:     check_at = created_at || current_time
595: 
596:     Hat.where(user_id: user.id, modlog_use: true).any? do |hat|
597:       check_at.between?(hat.created_at, hat.doffed_at || current_time)
598:     end
599:   end

607:     tags.each { |t| return false if t.privileged? }
608:     true

611:   # this has to happen just before save because it depends on user/editor
612:   def check_tags
613:     u = editor || user
614: 
615:     if u&.is_new? &&
616:         (unpermitted = tags.select { |t| t.permit_by_new_users == false }).any?
617:       tags_str = unpermitted.map(&:tag).to_sentence
618:       errors.add :base, "New users can't submit stories with the tag(s) #{tags_str}"
619:       self.new_user_acculturation_error = ModMail.create_inviter_discussion! user: u, message: <<~MESSAGE
620:         Hi,
621: 
622:         #{u.username}: New users can't submit stories with the tag(s) #{tags_str} because they're for meta discussion or prone to off-topic stories.
623:         If a tag is appropriate for the story, leaving the tag off to skirt this restriction can earn a ban.
624: 
625:         #{u.invited_by_user&.username}: This is a good opportunity for you introduce #{u.username} to what's [topical](https://lobste.rs/about#topicality) in the community.
626: 
627:         If you need, you can talk to the mods in this conversation, but we'll probably stay out of it unless asked.
628: 
629:         #{submission_markdown}
630:       MESSAGE
631:       return
632:     end
633: 
634:     if (privileged = tags.select { |t| !t.can_be_applied_by?(u) }).any?
635:       raise "#{u.username} does not have permission to use privileged tag(s): #{privileged.map(&:tag).join(" ")}"
636:     end
637: 
638:     if tags.reject { |t| t.is_media? }.empty?
639:       errors.add(:base, "Must have at least one non-media (PDF, video) " \
640:         "tag.  If no tags apply to your content, it probably doesn't " \
641:         "belong here.")
642:     end
643:   end

654:   def description_or_story_text(chars = 0)
655:     s = if description.present?

657:     else

659:     end
660: 
661:     if chars > 0 && s.to_s.length > chars

664:     end

666:     HtmlEncoder.decode(s.to_s)

673:   def fix_bogus_chars
674:     # this is needlessly complicated to work around character encoding issues
675:     # that arise when doing just self.title.to_s.gsub(160.chr, "")
676:     self.title = title.to_s.chars.map { |chr|
677:       if chr.ord == 160
678:         " "
679:       else
680:         chr
681:       end
682:     }.join("")
683: 
684:     true
685:   end

691:   # TODO: race condition: if two votes arrive at the same time, the second one
692:   # won't take the first's score change into effect for calculated_hotness
693:   def update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
694:     self.score += score_delta
695:     self.flags += flag_delta
696:     Story.connection.execute <<~SQL
697:       UPDATE stories SET
698:         score = (
699:           select sum(vote) from votes where story_id = stories.id and comment_id is null and (
700:             (vote = 1) or
701:             (
702:               vote = -1 and
703:               not exists ( -- user didn't comment
704:                 select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
705:               )
706:             )
707:           )
708:         ),
709:         flags = (select count(*) from votes where story_id = stories.id and comment_id is null and
710:           vote = -1 and
711:           not exists ( -- user didn't comment
712:             select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
713:           )
714:         ),
715:         hotness = #{calculated_hotness}
716:       WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
717:     SQL
718:   end

795:   def log_moderation
796:     if new_record? || (!editing_from_suggestions && (!editor || editor.id == user_id))
797:       return
798:     end
799: 
800:     all_changes = changes.merge(tag_changes)
801:     all_changes.delete("normalized_url")
802:     all_changes.delete("unavailable_at")
803:     all_changes.delete("last_edited_at")
804: 
805:     if !all_changes.any?
806:       return
807:     end
808: 
809:     m = Moderation.new
810:     if editing_from_suggestions
811:       m.is_from_suggestions = true
812:     else
813:       m.moderator_user_id = editor.try(:id)
814:     end
815:     m.story_id = id
816: 
817:     m.action = all_changes.map { |k, v|
818:       if k == "is_deleted" && is_deleted?
819:         "deleted story"
820:       elsif k == "is_deleted" && !is_deleted?
821:         "undeleted story"
822:       elsif k == "merged_story_id"
823:         if v[1]
824:           "merged into #{merged_into_story.short_id} " \
825:             "(#{merged_into_story.title})"
826:         else
827:           "unmerged from another story"
828:         end
829:       elsif v[0].is_a?(String) && v[1].is_a?(String) && v[0].present? && v[1].blank?
830:         "blanked #{k} from #{v[0].inspect}"
831:       else
832:         "changed #{k} from #{v[0].inspect} to #{v[1].inspect}"
833:       end
834:     }.join(", ")
835: 
836:     m.reason = moderation_reason
837:     m.save!
838: 
839:     self.is_moderated = true
840:   end

875:   def tag_changes
876:     # 'tags_was' is bad grammar but is named to mirror ActiveModel::Dirty's convention of providing
877:     # a 'attribute_was' reader. The AR associations API is pretty broad, so rather than try to
878:     # override all the methods to maintain state, this exception should prompt you to only use
879:     # the tags= method. See StoriesController#update for an example.
880:     raise "Controller didn't save tags_was for edit logging" if tags_was.nil?
881: 
882:     old_tag_names = tags_was.map(&:tag).join(" ")
883:     new_tag_names = tags.map(&:tag).join(" ")
884: 
885:     if old_tag_names == new_tag_names
886:       {}
887:     else
888:       {"tags" => [old_tag_names, new_tag_names]}
889:     end
890:   end

892:   def save_suggested_tags_for_user!(new_tag_names_a, user)
893:     suggested_taggings.where(user_id: user.id).delete_all
894: 
895:     new_suggested_tags = Tag
896:       .active
897:       .where(tag: new_tag_names_a.uniq.compact_blank)
898:       .map { |t| {user: user, story: self, tag: t} }
899:     SuggestedTagging.create!(new_suggested_tags)
900: 
901:     # if enough users voted on the same set of replacement tags, do it
902:     tag_votes = {}
903:     suggested_taggings.group_by(&:user_id).each do |_u, stg|
904:       stg.each do |s|
905:         tag_votes[s.tag.tag] ||= 0
906:         tag_votes[s.tag.tag] += 1
907:       end
908:     end
909: 
910:     final_tags = []
911:     tag_votes.each do |k, v|
912:       if v >= SUGGESTION_QUORUM
913:         final_tags.push k
914:       end
915:     end
916: 
917:     if final_tags.any? && (final_tags.sort != tags.map(&:tag).sort)
918:       # Rails.logger.info "[s#{id}] promoting suggested tags " \
919:       #  "#{final_tags.inspect} instead of #{tags.map(&:tag).inspect}"
920:       self.editor = nil
921:       self.editing_from_suggestions = true
922:       self.moderation_reason = "Automatically changed from user suggestions"
923:       self.tags_was = tags.to_a
924:       Story.transaction do
925:         self.tags = Tag.where(tag: final_tags)
926:         save!
927:       end
928:     end
929:   end

931:   def save_suggested_title_for_user!(title, user)
932:     st = suggested_titles.find_by(user_id: user.id)
933:     if !st
934:       st = suggested_titles.build
935:       st.user_id = user.id
936:     end
937:     st.title = title
938:     st.save!
939: 
940:     # if enough users voted on the same exact title, save it
941:     title_votes = {}
942:     suggested_titles.each do |s|
943:       title_votes[s.title] ||= 0
944:       title_votes[s.title] += 1
945:     end
946: 
947:     title_votes.sort_by { |_k, v| v }.reverse_each do |kv|
948:       if kv[1] >= SUGGESTION_QUORUM
949:         # Rails.logger.info "[s#{id}] promoting suggested title " \
950:         #   "#{kv[0].inspect} instead of #{self.title.inspect}"
951:         self.editor = nil
952:         self.editing_from_suggestions = true
953:         self.moderation_reason = "Automatically changed from user suggestions"
954:         self.title = kv[0]
955:         self.tags_was = tags.to_a
956:         if !save
957:           # Rails.logger.error "[s#{id}] failed auto promoting: " << errors.inspect
958:         end
959: 
960:         break
961:       end
962:     end
963:   end

1059:   def title=(t)

1064:   def title_as_slug
1065:     max_len = 35
1066:     wl = 0
1067:     words = []
1068: 
1069:     title
1070:       .parameterize
1071:       .gsub(/[^a-z0-9]/, "_")
1072:       .split("_")
1073:       .reject { |z| TITLE_DROP_WORDS.include?(z) }
1074:       .each do |w|
1075:       if wl + w.length <= max_len
1076:         words.push w
1077:         wl += w.length
1078:       else
1079:         if wl == 0
1080:           words.push w[0, max_len]
1081:         end
1082:         break
1083:       end
1084:     end
1085: 
1086:     if words.empty?
1087:       words.push "_"
1088:     end
1089: 
1090:     words.join("_").gsub("_-_", "-")
1091:   end

1157:   def set_domain_and_origin(domain_name)
1158:     domain_name&.sub!(/\Awww\d*\.(.+?\..+)/, '\1') # remove www\d* from domain if the url is not like www10.org
1159:     if domain_name.present?
1160:       self.domain = Domain.where(domain: domain_name).first_or_initialize
1161:       self.origin = domain&.find_or_create_origin(url)
1162:     else
1163:       self.domain = nil
1164:       self.origin = nil
1165:     end
1166:   end

1168:   def url=(u)
1169:     return if u.blank?
1170:     u = u.strip
1171: 
1172:     # strip out tracking query params
1173:     if (match = u.match(/\A([^?]+)\?(.+)\z/))
1174:       params = match[2].split(/[&?]/)
1175:       # utm_ is google and many others; sk is medium; si/pp is youtube
1176:       params.reject! { |p|
1177:         p.match(/^utm_(source|medium|campaign|term|content|referrer)=|^sk=|^gclid=|^fbclid=|^linkId=|^pp=|^si=|^trk=/x)
1178:       }
1179:       params.reject! { |p|
1180:         if /^lobsters|^src=lobsters|^ref=lobsters/x.match?(p)
1181:           ModNote.tattle_on_traffic_attribution!(self)
1182:           true
1183:         end
1184:       }
1185:       u = match[1] << (params.any? ? "?#{params.join("&")}" : "")
1186:     end
1187: 
1188:     if (match = u.match(Utils::URL_RE))
1189:       # remove well-known port for http and https if present
1190:       @url_port = match[:port]
1191:       if match[:protocol] == "http" && match[:port] == ":80" ||
1192:           match[:protocol] == "https" && match[:port] == ":443"
1193:         u = u[0...match.begin(3)] + u[match.end(3)..]
1194:         @url_port = nil
1195:       end
1196:     end
1197: 
1198:     # set field
1199:     super
1200: 
1201:     # set related fields
1202:     self.normalized_url = Utils.normalize(u)
1203:     set_domain_and_origin(match&.[](:domain))
1204:   end

1229:   def vote_summary_for(user)
1230:     r_counts = {}
1231:     r_whos = {}
1232:     votes.each do |v|
1233:       next if v.vote == 0
1234:       r_counts[v.reason.to_s] ||= 0
1235:       r_counts[v.reason.to_s] += 1
1236:       if user&.is_moderator?
1237:         r_whos[v.reason.to_s] ||= []
1238:         r_whos[v.reason.to_s].push v.user.username
1239:       end
1240:     end
1241: 
1242:     r_counts.keys.sort.map { |k|
1243:       if k == ""
1244:         "+#{r_counts[k]}"
1245:       else
1246:         "#{r_counts[k]} " +
1247:           (Vote::ALL_STORY_REASONS[k] || k) +
1248:           ((user && user.is_moderator?) ? " (#{r_whos[k].join(", ")})" : "")
1249:       end
1250:     }.join(", ")
1251:   end

1253:   def fetched_attributes_html
1254:     converted = @fetched_response.body.force_encoding("utf-8")
1255:     parsed = Nokogiri::HTML(converted.to_s)
1256: 
1257:     # parse best title from html tags
1258:     # try <meta property="og:title"> first, it probably won't have the site
1259:     # name
1260:     title = ""
1261:     begin
1262:       title = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:title']")
1263:         .attributes["content"].text
1264:     rescue
1265:     end
1266: 
1267:     # then try <meta name="title">
1268:     if title.to_s == ""
1269:       begin
1270:         title = parsed.at_css("meta[name='title']").attributes["content"].text
1271:       rescue
1272:       end
1273:     end
1274: 
1275:     # then try plain old <title>
1276:     if title.to_s == ""
1277:       title = parsed.at_css("title").try(:text).to_s
1278:     end
1279: 
1280:     # see if the site name is available, so we can strip it out in case it was
1281:     # present in the fetched title
1282:     begin
1283:       site_name = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:site_name']")
1284:         .attributes["content"].text
1285: 
1286:       if site_name.present? &&
1287:           site_name.length < title.length &&
1288:           title[-site_name.length, site_name.length] == site_name
1289:         title = title[0, title.length - site_name.length]
1290: 
1291:         # remove title/site name separator
1292:         if / [ \-|\u2013] $/.match?(title)
1293:           title = title[0, title.length - 3]
1294:         end
1295:       end
1296:     rescue
1297:     end
1298: 
1299:     @fetched_attributes[:title] = title
1300: 
1301:     # strip off common GitHub site + repo owner
1302:     @fetched_attributes[:title].sub!(/GitHub - [a-z\d](?:[a-z\d]|-(?=[a-z\d])){0,38}\//i, "")
1303: 
1304:     # attempt to get the canonical url if it can be parsed,
1305:     # if it is not the domain root path, and if it
1306:     # responds to GET with a 200-level code
1307:     begin
1308:       cu = canonical_target(parsed)
1309:       @fetched_attributes[:url] = cu if valid_canonical_uri?(cu)
1310:     rescue
1311:     end
1312: 
1313:     @fetched_attributes
1314:   end
1315: 
1316:   def fetched_attributes_pdf
1317:     return @fetched_attributes = {} if @fetched_response.body.length >= 5.megabytes
1318: 
1319:     # pdf-reader only accepts a stream or filename
1320:     pdf_stream = StringIO.new(@fetched_response.body)
1321:     pdf = PDF::Reader.new(pdf_stream)
1322: 
1323:     title = pdf.info[:Title]
1324: 
1325:     @fetched_attributes[:title] = title
1326:     @fetched_attributes
1327:   end
1328: 
1329:   def fetched_attributes
1330:     return @fetched_attributes if @fetched_attributes
1331: 
1332:     @fetched_attributes = {
1333:       url: url,
1334:       title: ""
1335:     }
1336: 
1337:     # security: do not connect to arbitrary user-submitted ports
1338:     return @fetched_attributes if @url_port
1339: 
1340:     begin
1341:       # if we haven't had a test inject a response into us
1342:       if !@fetched_response
1343:         s = Sponge.new
1344:         s.timeout = 3
1345:         # User submitted URLs may have an incorrect https certificate, but we
1346:         # don't want to fail the retrieval for this. Security risk is minimal.
1347:         s.ssl_verify = false
1348:         headers = {
1349:           "User-agent" => "#{Rails.application.domain} for #{fetching_ip}",
1350:           "Referer" => Rails.application.domain
1351:         }
1352:         res = s.fetch(url, :get, nil, nil, headers, 3)
1353:         @fetched_response = res
1354:       end
1355: 
1356:       case @fetched_response["content-type"]
1357:       when /pdf/
1358:         fetched_attributes_pdf
1359:       else
1360:         fetched_attributes_html
1361:       end
1362:     rescue
1363:       @fetched_attributes
1364:     end
1365:   end
1366: 
1367:   private
1368: 
1369:   def valid_canonical_uri?(url)
1370:     ucu = URI.parse(url)
1371:     new_page = ucu &&
1372:       ucu.scheme.present? &&
1373:       ucu.host.present? &&
1374:       ucu.path != "/"
1375: 
1376:     return false unless new_page
1377: 
1378:     res = Sponge.new.fetch(url)
1379: 
1380:     res.code.to_s =~ /\A2.*\Z/
1381:   end
1382: 
1383:   def canonical_target(parsed)
1384:     parsed.at_css("link[rel='canonical']").attributes["href"].text

1: # typed: false
2: 
3: class Vote < ApplicationRecord
4:   belongs_to :user
5:   belongs_to :story
6:   belongs_to :comment, optional: true
7: 
8:   normalizes :reason, with: ->(r) { r.to_s }, apply_to_nil: true
9: 
10:   # for comment_vote_summaries
11:   attribute :count, :integer
12:   attribute :usernames, :string
13: 
14:   validates :vote, presence: true, inclusion: {in: [1, -1]}
15:   validates :reason,
16:     length: {is: 1},
17:     allow_blank: true,
18:     presence: true
19:   validate :validate_flagger_hasnt_replied
20: 
21:   scope :comments_flags, ->(comments, user = nil) {
22:     q = where(comment: comments, vote: -1)
23:     user ? q.where(user: user) : q.all
24:   }
25: 
26:   # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
27:   COMMENT_REASONS = {
28:     "O" => "Off-topic",
29:     "M" => "Me-too",
30:     "T" => "Troll",
31:     "U" => "Unkind",
32:     "S" => "Spam",
33:     "" => "Cancel"
34:   }.freeze
35:   ALL_COMMENT_REASONS = COMMENT_REASONS.merge({
36:     "I" => "Incorrect"
37:   }).freeze
38: 
39:   # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
40:   STORY_REASONS = {
41:     "O" => "Off-topic",
42:     "A" => "Already Posted",
43:     "B" => "Broken Link",
44:     "S" => "Spam",
45:     "" => "Cancel"
46:   }.freeze
47:   ALL_STORY_REASONS = STORY_REASONS.merge({
48:     "Q" => "Low Quality"
49:   }).freeze
50: 
51:   def on_comment?
52:     comment_id.present?
53:   end
54: 
55:   def on_story?
56:     comment_id.blank?
57:   end
58: 
59:   def reason_text
60:     if on_story?
61:       ALL_STORY_REASONS[reason]
62:     else
63:       ALL_COMMENT_REASONS[reason]
64:     end
65:   end
66: 
67:   def self.comment_vote_summaries(comment_ids)
68:     Vote
69:       .joins(:user)
70:       .select("comment_id, reason, count(1) as count, group_concat(username separator ', ') as usernames")
71:       .where(comment_id: comment_ids)
72:       .where.not(reason: "")
73:       .group(:comment_id, :reason)
74:       .group_by(&:comment_id)
75:   end
76: 
77:   def self.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(user, stories)
78:     votes = {}
79: 
80:     Vote.where(user_id: user, story_id: stories,
81:       comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
82:       votes[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
83:     end
84: 
85:     votes
86:   end
87: 
88:   def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_story_hash(user_id, story_id)
89:     votes = {}
90: 
91:     Vote.where(
92:       user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id
93:     ).where.not(comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
94:       votes[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
95:     end
96: 
97:     votes
98:   end
99: 
100:   def self.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(user_id, story_ids)
101:     if story_ids.empty?
102:       {}
103:     else
104:       votes = where(
105:         user_id: user_id,
106:         comment_id: nil,
107:         story_id: story_ids
108:       )
109:       votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
110:         memo[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
111:       end
112:     end
113:   end
114: 
115:   def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_comment_ids_hash(user_id, comment_ids)
116:     return {} if user_id.nil? || comment_ids.empty?
117: 
118:     votes = where(
119:       user_id: user_id,
120:       comment_id: comment_ids
121:     ).select(:comment_id, :vote, :reason)
122:     votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
123:       memo[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
124:     end
125:   end
126: 
127:   def self.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
128:     new_vote, story_id, comment_id, user_id, reason, update_counters = true
129:   )
130:     v = Vote.where(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id,
131:       comment_id: comment_id).first_or_initialize
132: 
133:     # AR wraps a transaction around INSERTing to votes. I don't know why, but it also SELECTs the
134:     # associated story even though there isn't a touch: or callback on the association. This can
135:     # lead to a rare deadlock if someone is creating a story at the some time, because that has to
136:     # lock the stories table and then votes. This next line eagerly loads the associated story so
137:     # the SELECT doesn't happen during the transaction INSERTing to votes. Oddly, this doesn't
138:     # happen with associated comments.
139:     v.story if story_id.present?
140: 
141:     return if !v.new_record? && v.vote == new_vote # done if there's no change
142: 
143:     # score deltas when flags no longer affect scores
144:     # no vote  -> 1      1
145:     # flag -> 1          1
146:     # no vote  -> flag   0
147:     # flag -> no vote    0
148:     # 1  -> no vote     -1
149:     # 1  -> flag        -1
150:     score_delta = if new_vote == 1
151:       1
152:     elsif v.vote == 1
153:       -1
154:     else
155:       0
156:     end
157:     flag_delta = if v.vote == -1
158:       # we know there's a change, so we must be removing a flag
159:       -1
160:     elsif new_vote == -1
161:       # we know there's a change, so we must be adding a flag
162:       1
163:     else
164:       # change from 1 to 0 or 0 to 1, so number of flags doesn't change
165:       0
166:     end
167: 
168:     if new_vote == 0
169:       v.destroy!
170:     else
171:       v.vote = new_vote
172:       v.reason = reason
173:       v.save!
174:     end
175: 
176:     if update_counters
177:       t = v.target
178:       if v.user_id != t.user_id
179:         User.update_counters t.user_id, karma: score_delta
180:       end
181:       t.update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
182:     end
183:   end
184: 
185:   def target
186:     if comment_id
187:       Comment.find(comment_id)
188:     else
189:       Story.find(story_id)
190:     end
191:   end
192: 
193:   def validate_flagger_hasnt_replied
194:     if vote == -1 && comment_id && Comment.where(user_id: user_id, parent_comment_id: comment_id).exists?
195:       errors.add(:base, "You've already replied to the comment.")
196:     end
197:   end
198: end

3: class Link < ApplicationRecord
4:   belongs_to :from_story, class_name: "Story", optional: true
5:   belongs_to :from_comment, class_name: "Comment", optional: true
6:   belongs_to :to_story, class_name: "Story", optional: true
7:   belongs_to :to_comment, class_name: "Comment", optional: true
8: 
9:   validates :url, length: {maximum: 250, allow_nil: false}, presence: true
10:   validate :valid_url
11:   validates :normalized_url, length: {maximum: 255, allow_nil: false}, presence: true
12:   validates :title, length: {maximum: 255}
13:   validate :validate_from_presence_xor
14:   validate :validate_only_one_to
15: 
16:   # rubocop:disable Rails/UniqueValidationWithoutIndex
17:   # no database-level index because, per https://mariadb.com/kb/en/getting-started-with-indexes/
18:   # > In SQL any NULL is never equal to anything, not even to another NULL. Consequently, a UNIQUE
19:   # > constraint will not prevent one from storing duplicate rows if they contain null values:
20:   validates :url, uniqueness: {scope: [:from_story, :from_comment]}
21:   validates :to_comment, uniqueness: {scope: [:from_story, :from_comment]}, if: ->(l) { l.to_comment.present? }
22:   validates :to_story, uniqueness: {scope: [:from_story, :from_comment]}, if: ->(l) { l.to_story.present? }
23:   # rubocop:enable Rails/UniqueValidationWithoutIndex
24: 
25:   scope :recently_linked_from_comments, ->(url) {
26:     joins(:from_comment).includes(:from_comment)
27:       .where(from_comment: {created_at: (7.days.ago)..})
28:       .where(normalized_url: Utils.normalize(url))
29:   }
30: 
31:   def url=(u)
32:     return if u.blank?
33: 
34:     super(u.to_s.strip)
35:     self.normalized_url = Utils.normalize(url)
36: 
37:     if normalized_url.starts_with? Rails.application.domain
38:       path = normalized_url.delete_prefix(Rails.application.domain)
39:       r = Rails.application.routes.recognize_path(path, method: :get)
40: 
41:       if r[:controller] == "comments" && %w[show redirect_from_short_id show_short_id].include?(r[:action])
42:         self.to_comment = Comment.find_by(short_id: r[:id])
43:       elsif r[:controller] == "stories" && r[:action] == "show"
44:         # check if the url ends with a comment anchor
45:         if (m = url.to_s.match(/#c_([0-9A-Za-z]{6,8}\z)/))
46:           self.to_comment = Comment.find_by(short_id: m[1])
47:         else
48:           self.to_story = Story.find_by(short_id: r[:id])
49:         end
50:       end
51:     end
52:   rescue ActionController::RoutingError
53:     # ignore invalid URLs from recognize_path
54:   end
55: 
56:   # acts idempotently, ignoring multiple Links to the same url/story/comment
57:   # ignores validation errors; some comments have bad links ('#', 'https://foo/', 'https://http://apple.com')
58:   def self.recreate_from_comment! c
59:     Link.transaction do
60:       Link.where(from_comment_id: c.id).delete_all
61:       c.parsed_links.each(&:save)
62:     end
63:   end
64: 
65:   # acts idempotently, ignoring multiple Links to the same url/story/comment
66:   def self.recreate_from_story! s
67:     Link.transaction do
68:       Link.where(from_story_id: s.id).delete_all
69:       s.parsed_links.each(&:save)
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   # Recalculates the cached normalized URL for each link, should be run whenever
74:   # Utils.normalize changes so that new story submissions can locate links in old
75:   # discussions correctly.
76:   def self.refresh_normalized_urls!
77:     # Note that we assume normalization doesn't affect whether a URL links to a comment or story
78:     # on site, as calculated in url=
79:     Link.find_each { |l| l.update_columns normalized_url: Utils.normalize(l.url) }
80:   end
81: 
82:   private
83: 
84:   def valid_url
85:     errors.add(:url, "is not valid") unless url.match?(Utils::URL_RE)
86:   end
87: 
88:   def validate_from_presence_xor
89:     errors.add(:base, "from_story xor from_comment must be present") unless from_story.present? ^ from_comment.present?
90:   end
91: 
92:   def validate_only_one_to
93:     errors.add(:base, "Both to_story and to_comment cannot be set") if to_story.present? && to_comment.present?
94:   end
95: end

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/sense/lobsters/app/models/search.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # typed: false
2: 
3: # results:
4: #   not performed
5: #   nothing found
6: #   invalid: negative w/o positive term
7: #   invalid: multiple domains
8: #   invalid: unknown tag
9: #   results
10: 
11: class Search
12:   attr_reader :q, :what, :order, :page, :searcher
13:   attr_reader :invalid_because, :parse_tree
14: 
15:   # takes untrusted params from controller, so sanitize
16:   def initialize params, user
17:     @q = params[:q]
18:     @parse_tree = if params[:q].present?
19:       SearchParser.new.parse(params[:q])
20:     else
21:       []
22:     end
23: 
24:     @what = if %w[stories comments].include? params[:what]
25:       params[:what].to_sym
26:     else
27:       :comments
28:     end
29: 
30:     @order = if %w[newest relevance score].include? params[:order]
31:       params[:order].to_sym
32:     else
33:       :newest
34:     end
35: 
36:     @page = params[:page].to_i
37:     @page = 1 if @page == 0
38: 
39:     @searcher = user
40: 
41:     @results = nil
42:     @results_count = params[:results_count] || -1
43:     @invalid_because = nil
44:   end
45: 
46:   # returns @results so perform_* can return from calling this
47:   def invalid reason
48:     @invalid_because = reason
49:     @results_count = 0
50:     @results = searched_model.none
51:   end
52: 
53:   def max_matches
54:     per_page * 20
55:   end
56: 
57:   def per_page
58:     20
59:   end
60: 
61:   def to_param
62:     {
63:       q: @q,
64:       what: @what,
65:       order: @order,
66:       page: @page
67:     }
68:   end
69: 
70:   def page_count
71:     total = results_count.to_i
72: 
73:     if total == -1 || total > max_matches
74:       total = max_matches
75:     end
76: 
77:     ((total - 1) / per_page.to_i) + 1
78:   end
79: 
80:   def perform!
81:     return (@results = searched_model.none) if q.blank? || parse_tree.blank?
82:     if what == :stories
83:       perform_story_search!
84:     else
85:       perform_comment_search!
86:     end
87:   end
88: 
89:   # security: must prevent sql injection
90:   # it assumes SearchParser prevents "
91:   def flatten_title tree
92:     if tree.keys.first == :term
93:       ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote_string(tree.values.first.to_s)
94:     elsif tree.keys.first == :quoted
95:       '"' + tree.values.first.map(&:values).flatten.join(" ").gsub("'", "\\\\'") + '"'
96:     end
97:   end
98: 
99:   # security: must prevent sql injection
100:   # strip all nonword except -_' so people can search for contractions like "don't"
101:   # some of these are search operators, some sql injection
102:   # https://mariadb.com/kb/ru/full-text-index-overview/#in-boolean-mode
103:   # surprise: + is not in \p{Punct}
104:   def strip_operators s
105:     s.to_s
106:       .gsub(/[^\p{Word}']/, " ")
107:       .gsub("'", "\\\\'")
108:       .strip
109:   end
110: 
111:   # not security-sensitive, mariadb ignores 1 and 2 character terms and
112:   # stripping them allows the frontend to explain they're ignored
113:   def strip_short_terms(s)
114:     s.to_s.strip.split(/\p{Space}/).filter { it.size > 2 }.join(" ")
115:   end
116: 
117:   def perform_comment_search!
118:     query = Comment.accessible_to_user(searcher).for_presentation
119: 
120:     terms = []
121:     n_commenters = 0
122:     n_domains = 0
123:     n_submitters = 0
124:     n_tags = 0
125:     tags = nil
126:     url = false
127:     title = false
128: 
129:     # array of hashes, type => value(s)
130:     parse_tree.each do |node|
131:       type, value = node.first
132:       case type
133:       when :commenter, :user
134:         n_commenters += 1
135:         return invalid("A comment only has one commenter") if n_commenters > 1
136:         query.joins!(:user).where!(users: {username: value.to_s})
137:       when :domain
138:         n_domains += 1
139:         return invalid("A story can't be from multiple domains at once") if n_domains > 1
140:         query.joins!(story: [:domain]).where!(story: {domains: {domain: value.to_s}})
141:       when :submitter
142:         n_submitters += 1
143:         return invalid("A story only has one submitter") if n_submitters > 1
144:         query.joins!(story: :user).where!(story: {users: {username: value.to_s}})
145:       when :tag
146:         n_tags += 1
147:         tags ||= Tag.none.select(:id)
148:         tags.or!(Tag.where(tag: value.to_s))
149:       # TODO unknown tag
150:       when :title
151:         title = true
152:         value = flatten_title value
153:         query.where!(
154:           story: Story.joins(:story_text).where(
155:             "MATCH(story_texts.title) AGAINST ('+#{value}' in boolean mode)"
156:           )
157:         )
158:       when :url
159:         url = true
160:         query
161:           .joins!(:story)
162:           .and!(
163:             Story.where(url: value.to_s)
164:               .or(Story.where(normalized_url: Utils.normalize(value)))
165:           )
166:       when :negated
167:         # TODO
168:       when :quoted
169:         terms.append '"' + strip_operators(value.pluck(:term).join(" ")) + '"'
170:       # no 'when' for stopword or shortword, they're not searchable
171:       when :term, :catchall
172:         val = strip_short_terms(strip_operators(value))
173:         # if punctuation is replaced with a space, this would generate a terms search
174:         # AGAINST('+' in boolean mode)
175:         terms.append val if !val.empty?
176:       end
177:     end
178:     if terms.any?
179:       terms_sql = <<~SQL.tr("\n", " ")
180:         MATCH(comment)
181:         AGAINST ('#{terms.map { |s| "+#{s}" }.join(" ")}' in boolean mode)
182:       SQL
183:       query.where! terms_sql
184:     end
185:     if tags
186:       query.where!(
187:         story: Story
188:                 .joins(:tags)
189:                 .where(tags: tags)
190:                 .group("stories.id")
191:                 .having("count(distinct tags.id) = #{n_tags}")
192:       )
193:     end
194: 
195:     # don't allow blank searches for all records when strip_ removes all data
196:     if n_commenters == 0 &&
197:         n_domains == 0 &&
198:         n_submitters == 0 &&
199:         n_tags == 0 &&
200:         !url &&
201:         !title &&
202:         terms.empty?
203:       return invalid("No search terms recognized")
204:     end
205: 
206:     @results_count = query.dup.count
207:     # with_tags uses group_by, so count returns a hash
208:     @results_count = @results_count.count if @results_count.is_a? Hash
209: 
210:     case order
211:     when :newest
212:       query.order!(id: :desc)
213:     when :relevance
214:       # relevance is undefined without search terms so sort by score
215:       if terms.any?
216:         query.order!(Arel.sql(terms_sql + " DESC"))
217:       else
218:         query.order!(score: :desc)
219:       end
220:     when :score
221:       query.order!(score: :desc)
222:     end
223: 
224:     query.limit!(per_page)
225:     query.offset!((page - 1) * per_page)
226:     query
227:   end
228: 
229:   def perform_story_search!
230:     query = Story.base(@searcher).for_presentation
231: 
232:     terms = []
233:     n_domains = 0
234:     n_submitters = 0
235:     n_tags = 0
236:     tags = nil
237:     url = false
238:     title = false
239: 
240:     # array of hashes, type => value(s)
241:     parse_tree.each do |node|
242:       type, value = node.first
243:       case type
244:       when :commenter
245:         return invalid("Doesn't make sense to search Stories by commenter")
246:       when :domain
247:         n_domains += 1
248:         return invalid("A story can't be from multiple domains at once") if n_domains > 1
249:         query.joins!(:domain).where!(domains: {domain: value.to_s})
250:       when :submitter, :user
251:         n_submitters += 1
252:         return invalid("A story only has one submitter") if n_submitters > 1
253:         query.joins!(:user).where!(user: {username: value.to_s})
254:       when :tag
255:         n_tags += 1
256:         tags ||= Tag.none.select(:id)
257:         tags.or!(Tag.where(tag: value.to_s))
258:       # TODO unknown tag
259:       when :title
260:         title = true
261:         value = flatten_title value
262:         query.joins!(:story_text).where!(
263:           "MATCH(story_texts.title) AGAINST ('+#{value}' in boolean mode)"
264:         )
265:       when :url
266:         url = true
267:         query.and!(
268:           Story.where(url: value.to_s)
269:             .or(Story.where(normalized_url: Utils.normalize(value)))
270:         )
271:       when :negated
272:         # TODO
273:       when :quoted
274:         terms.append '"' + strip_operators(value.pluck(:term).join(" ")) + '"'
275:       # no 'when' for stopword or shortword, they're not searchable
276:       when :term, :catchall
277:         val = strip_short_terms(strip_operators(value))
278:         # if punctuation is replaced with a space, this would generate a terms search
279:         # AGAINST('+' in boolean mode)
280:         terms.append val if !val.empty?
281:       end
282:     end
283:     if terms.any?
284:       terms_sql = <<~SQL.tr("\n", " ")
285:         MATCH(story_texts.title, story_texts.description, story_texts.body)
286:         AGAINST ('#{terms.map { |s| "+#{s}" }.join(" ")}' in boolean mode)
287:       SQL
288:       query.joins!(:story_text).where! terms_sql
289:     end
290:     if tags
291:       # This searches tags by subquery because otherwise Rails recognizes the join against tags and
292:       # thinks the .tags association preload is satisfied, so returned stories will only have the
293:       # searched-for tags.
294:       query.joins!(<<~SQL.tr("\n", "")
295:         inner join (
296:           select stories.id
297:           from stories
298:           join taggings on taggings.story_id = stories.id
299:           where taggings.tag_id in (#{tags.to_sql})
300:           group by stories.id
301:           having count(distinct taggings.id) = #{n_tags}
302:         ) as stories_with_tags on stories_with_tags.id = stories.id
303:       SQL
304:                   )
305:     end
306: 
307:     # don't allow blank searches for all records when strip_ removes all data
308:     if n_domains == 0 &&
309:         n_submitters == 0 &&
310:         n_tags == 0 &&
311:         !url &&
312:         !title &&
313:         terms.empty?
314:       return invalid("No search terms recognized")
315:     end
316: 
317:     @results_count = query.dup.count
318: 
319:     case order
320:     when :newest
321:       query.order!(id: :desc)
322:     when :relevance
323:       # relevance is undefined without search terms so sort by score
324:       if terms.any?
325:         query.order!(Arel.sql(terms_sql + " desc"))
326:       else
327:         query.order!(score: :desc)
328:       end
329:     when :score
330:       query.order!(score: :desc)
331:     end
332: 
333:     query.limit!(per_page)
334:     query.offset!((page - 1) * per_page)
335:     query
336:   end
337: 
338:   def results
339:     @results ||= perform!
340:   end
341: 
342:   def results_count
343:     perform! if @results.nil?
344:     @results_count
345:   end
346: 
347:   def searched_model
348:     (what == :stories) ? Story : Comment
349:   end
350: end

(End of file - total 350 lines)

1: # typed: false
2: 
3: class InboxMailbox < ApplicationMailbox
4:   before_processing :required_info
5: 
6:   def process
7:     c = Comment.new(user: sending_user, comment: @decoded, is_from_email: true)
8:     if parent.is_a?(Comment)
9:       c.story_id = parent.story_id
10:       c.parent_comment_id = parent.id
11:     else
12:       c.story_id = parent.id
13:     end
14: 
15:     # If we fail to parse the comment, just throw an exception.
16:     c.save!
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def forcibly_convert_to_utf8(string)
22:     if string.encoding == Encoding::UTF_8 && string.valid_encoding?
23:       return string
24:     end
25: 
26:     string.b.encode(
27:       Encoding::UTF_8,
28:       invalid: :replace,
29:       undef: :replace,
30:       replace: "?"
31:     )
32:   end
33: 
34:   def parent
35:     return @parent if @parent
36: 
37:     # Emails In-Reply-To formatted like
38:     # story.<short_id>.<timestamp of story creation>@<app domain>
39:     # We throw away the timestamp, since we only care about the short ID.
40:     irt = mail.in_reply_to.to_s.gsub(/[^A-Za-z0-9@.]/, "")
41: 
42:     if (m = irt.match(/^comment\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
43:       @parent = Comment.where(short_id: m[1]).first
44:     elsif (m = irt.match(/^story\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
45:       @parent = Story.where(short_id: m[1]).first
46:     end
47: 
48:     @parent
49:   end
50: 
51:   def required_info
52:     body = if mail.content_type.blank? # old, non-multipart message
53:       mail.decoded.to_s
54:     elsif /text\/plain/.match?(mail.content_type.to_s)
55:       mail.body.to_s
56:     elsif mail.multipart?
57:       # parts[0] - multipart/alternative
58:       #  parts[0].parts[0] - text/plain
59:       #  parts[0].parts[1] - text/html
60:       if (found = mail.parts.first.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }
61:          ).any?
62:         found.first.body.to_s
63:       elsif (found = mail.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }).any?
64:         found.first.body.to_s
65:       else
66:         bounced!
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     @decoded = tidy(forcibly_convert_to_utf8(body))
71:     if @decoded == "" || sending_user.nil? || parent.nil?
72:       # We could email the user about the bounce, but that doesn't seem
73:       # to align with current behaviour.
74:       bounced!
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   def user_token
79:     (mail.to.find { |e| e =~ /^#{Rails.application.shortname}-/ } || "")[/-([^@]*)@/, 1]
80:   end
81: 
82:   def sending_user
83:     return @sending_user if @sending_user
84: 
85:     if (user = User.find_by(mailing_list_token: user_token)) &&
86:         user.is_active?
87:       @sending_user = user
88:       user
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   def tidy(body)
93:     body.gsub(/^-- \n.+\z/m, "")                # sig
94:       .gsub(/^(On|on|at) .*\n\n?(>.*\n)+/m, "") # top-posting
95:       .strip
96:   end
97: end

1: # typed: false
2: 
3: class Moderation < ApplicationRecord
4:   belongs_to :moderator,
5:     class_name: "User",
6:     foreign_key: "moderator_user_id",
7:     inverse_of: :moderations,
8:     optional: true
9:   belongs_to :comment,
10:     optional: true
11:   belongs_to :domain,
12:     optional: true
13:   belongs_to :origin,
14:     optional: true
15:   belongs_to :story,
16:     optional: true
17:   belongs_to :tag,
18:     optional: true
19:   belongs_to :user,
20:     optional: true
21:   belongs_to :category,
22:     optional: true
23: 
24:   scope :for_user, ->(user) {
25:     left_outer_joins(:story, :comment)
26:       .includes(:moderator, comment: [:user, :story], story: :user)
27:       .where("
28:         moderations.user_id = ? OR
29:         stories.user_id = ? OR
30:         comments.user_id = ?", user, user, user)
31:       .order(id: :desc)
32:       .limit(20)
33:   }
34:   scope :for_story, ->(story) {
35:     left_outer_joins(:story, :comment)
36:       .includes(:moderator, comment: [:user, :story], story: :user)
37:       .where("
38:         moderations.user_id = ? OR
39:         stories.user_id = ? OR
40:         comments.user_id = ? OR
41:         moderations.story_id = ? OR
42:         comments.story_id = ? ",
43:         story.user, story.user, story.user, story, story)
44:       .order(id: :desc)
45:       .limit(20)
46:   }
47: 
48:   include Token
49: 
50:   # A bug mistakenly recorded 10 users who doffed hats as moderators. To be able to say we don't
51:   # edit or remove modlog entries without adding a caveat, I've listed the tokens for those and the
52:   # frontend can explain that error when they appear. https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/issues/1591
53:   BAD_DOFFING_ENTRIES = %w[
54:     moderation_01j6ax48wpfb4vas8qgv8vmg20
55:     moderation_01j79djrajfa5a56bbpmb6msde
56:     moderation_01j8myn90rey8axkbw0hntxfqe
57:     moderation_01j8n06kdrf5n92txy802evnz3
58:     moderation_01jajsjdd5fva8qdwgmc2es9gp
59:     moderation_01jat93zq7fgmr8dm6fc411g2c
60:     moderation_01jdgt4btvfvear3hphy79104f
61:     moderation_01jhzbz2mafxrsbmmr3trh760c
62:     moderation_01jmsbwaqxf2bbwws92cv8688k
63:     moderation_01jnywh2rxekqa61seqmk8z3f7
64:   ].freeze
65: 
66:   validates :action, presence: true, length: {maximum: 16_777_215}
67:   validates :reason, length: {maximum: 16_777_215}
68:   validates :is_from_suggestions, inclusion: {in: [true, false]}
69:   validate :one_foreign_key_present
70: 
71:   after_create :send_message_to_moderated
72:   after_create -> { ModActivity.create_for! self }
73: 
74:   def send_message_to_moderated
75:     m = Message.new
76:     m.author_user_id = moderator_user_id
77: 
78:     # mark as deleted by author so they don't fill up moderator message boxes
79:     m.deleted_by_author = true
80: 
81:     if story
82:       m.recipient_user_id = story.user_id
83:       m.subject = "Your story has been edited by " +
84:         (is_from_suggestions? ? "user suggestions" : "a moderator")
85:       m.body = "Your story [#{story.title}](" \
86:         "#{Routes.title_url(story)}) has been edited with the following " \
87:         "changes:\n" \
88:         "\n" \
89:         "> *#{action}*\n"
90: 
91:       if reason.present?
92:         m.body << "\n" \
93:           "The reason given:\n" \
94:           "\n" \
95:           "> *#{reason}*\n" \
96:           "\n" \
97:           "Maybe the guidelines on topicality are useful: https://lobste.rs/about#topicality"
98:       end
99: 
100:     elsif comment
101:       m.recipient_user_id = comment.user_id
102:       m.subject = "Your comment has been moderated"
103:       m.body = "Your comment on [#{comment.story.title}](" \
104:         "#{Routes.title_url comment.story}) has been moderated:\n" \
105:         "\n" <<
106:         comment.comment.split("\n").map { |l| "> #{l}" }.join("\n")
107: 
108:       if reason.present?
109:         m.body << "\n" \
110:           "The reason given:\n" \
111:           "\n" \
112:           "> *#{reason}*\n"
113:       end
114: 
115:     else
116:       # no point in alerting deleted users, they can't login to read it
117:       return
118:     end
119: 
120:     return if m.recipient_user_id == m.author_user_id
121: 
122:     m.body << "\n" \
123:       "*This is an automated message.*"
124: 
125:     m.save!
126:   end
127: 
128:   protected
129: 
130:   def one_foreign_key_present
131:     fks = [comment_id, domain_id, origin_id, story_id, category_id, tag_id, user_id].compact.length
132:     errors.add(:base, "moderation should be linked to only one object") if fks != 1
133:   end
134: end

1: # typed: false
2: 
3: require "commonmarker"
4: 
5: class Markdowner
6:   COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS = {
7:     extension: {
8:       tagfilter: true,
9:       autolink: true,
10:       strikethrough: true,
11:       header_ids: nil,
12:       shortcodes: nil
13:     },
14:     render: {
15:       escape: true,
16:       hardbreaks: false,
17:       escaped_char_spans: false
18:     }
19:   }
20:   USERNAME_MENTION = /\B([@~]#{User::VALID_USERNAME})\b/o
21: 
22:   # Rerender all markdown (#1627) cached in db columns, a pattern that predates Rails frament
23:   # caching and our adoption of the feature (6ce15c4f in 2025-10). As we build confidence in
24:   # fragment caching we should drop these columns.
25:   def self.rerender_db_markdown!
26:     Comment.all.find_each { |c| c.update_columns markeddown_comment: Markdowner.to_html(c.comment, as_of: c.created_at) }
27:     ModNote.all.find_each { |n| n.update_columns markeddown_note: Markdowner.to_html(n.note, as_of: n.created_at) }
28:     Story.where.not(description: "")
29:       .find_each { |s|
30:         s.update_columns markeddown_description: Markdowner.to_html(
31:           s.description,
32:           allow_images: s.can_have_images?,
33:           as_of: s.created_at
34:         )
35:       }
36:   end
37: 
38:   # allow_images: transform ![](example.com/img.jpg) to a hotlinked image; default is to print a link to the URL
39:   # as_of: what timestamp to add @mention links as of, because users rename; default is nil which is overridden
40:   #        to Time.current for newly created models passing their nil created_at
41:   def self.to_html(text, allow_images: false, as_of: nil)
42:     if text.blank?
43:       return ""
44:     end
45: 
46:     as_of ||= Time.current
47: 
48:     root = Commonmarker.parse(text.to_s, options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS)
49: 
50:     walk_text_nodes(root) { |n| link_username_mentions(n, as_of:) }
51: 
52:     ng = Nokogiri::HTML(root.to_html(
53:       options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS,
54:       plugins: {syntax_highlighter: nil}
55:     ))
56: 
57:     # change <h1>, <h2>, etc. headings to just bold tags
58:     ng.css("h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6").each do |h|
59:       h.name = "strong"
60:     end
61: 
62:     # This should happen before adding rel=ugc to all links
63:     convert_images_to_links(ng) unless allow_images
64: 
65:     # make links have rel=ugc
66:     ng.css("a").each do |h|
67:       h[:rel] = "ugc"
68:     end
69: 
70:     if ng.at_css("body")
71:       ng.at_css("body").inner_html
72:     else
73:       ""
74:     end
75:   end
76: 
77:   def self.to_raw(text)
78:     if text.blank?
79:       return ""
80:     end
81: 
82:     root = Commonmarker.parse(text.to_s.dup.force_encoding("utf-8"), options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS)
83: 
84:     root.walk do |node|
85:       if node.type == :image ||
86:           node.type == :link ||
87:           node.type == :emph ||
88:           node.type == :strong ||
89:           node.type == :strikethrough
90:         node.insert_before(node.first_child)
91:         node.delete
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     root.to_commonmark
96:   end
97: 
98:   def self.walk_text_nodes(node, &block)
99:     return if node.type == :link
100:     return block.call(node) if node.type == :text
101: 
102:     node.each do |child|
103:       walk_text_nodes(child, &block)
104:     end
105:   end
106: 
107:   def self.link_username_mentions(node, as_of:)
108:     # This does 1+n queries in username linkification in comments/bios because this works inorder on
109:     # the parse tree rather than running once over the text. Proper fix: loop to find usernames, do
110:     # one lookup, then loop again to manipulate the nodes for usernames that exist.
111: 
112:     while node
113:       return unless node.string_content =~ USERNAME_MENTION
114:       before, user, after = $`, $1, $'
115: 
116:       node.string_content = before
117: 
118:       if Username.where("created_at < ? and (? < renamed_away_at or renamed_away_at is null)", as_of, as_of).exists?(username: user[1..])
119:         link = Commonmarker::Node.new(:link, url: Rails.application.root_url + "~#{user[1..]}")
120:         node.insert_after(link)
121: 
122:         link_text = Commonmarker::Node.new(:text)
123:         link_text.string_content = user
124:         link.append_child(link_text)
125: 
126:         node = link
127:       else
128:         node.string_content += user
129:       end
130: 
131:       if after.length > 0
132:         remainder = Commonmarker::Node.new(:text)
133:         remainder.string_content = after
134:         node.insert_after(remainder)
135: 
136:         node = remainder
137:       else
138:         node = nil
139:       end
140:     end
141:   end
142: 
143:   def self.convert_images_to_links(node)
144:     node.css("img").each do |img|
145:       link = node.create_element("a")
146: 
147:       link["href"], title, alt = img.attributes
148:         .values_at("src", "title", "alt")
149:         .map(&:to_s)
150: 
151:       link.content = [title, alt, link["href"]].find(&:present?)
152: 
153:       img.replace link
154:     end
155:   end
156: end

(End of file - total 156 lines)
