<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # typed: false
2: 
3: class Utils
4:   # URI.parse is not very lenient, so we can't use it
5:   # requires a . in domain because we don't want people submitting links to internal hostnames
6:   URL_RE = /\A(?<protocol>https?):\/\/(?<domain>(?:[^.\/]+\.)+[a-z-]+)(?<port>:\d+)?(?:\/(?:[\w.~:\/?#\[\]@!$&'()*+,;=%-]*))?\z/i
7: 
8:   # utility that works on stringlikes (for possibly invalid user input + searches)
9:   # If this function changes, you should run Story.refresh_normalized_urls! and Link.refresh_normalized_urls!
10:   def self.normalize(url)
11:     return url if url.blank?
12:     url = url.to_s.dup # in case passed a Url or frozen string
13: 
14:     url.slice! %r{#.*$} # remove anchor
15:     url.slice! %r{/$} # remove trailing slash
16:     url.slice! %r{\.html?$} # remove .htm, .html
17: 
18:     # remove some common "directory index" pages that are commonly served for dirs
19:     url.slice! %r{/index$} # includes index.html? from previous
20:     url.slice! %r{/index\.php}
21:     url.slice! %r{/Default\.aspx$}
22: 
23:     url.slice! %r{https?://} # consider http and https the same
24:     url.sub!(/\Awww\d*\.(.+?\..+)/, '\1') # remove www\d* from domain if the url is not like www10.org
25: 
26:     url, *args = url.split(/[&?]/) # trivia: ?a=1?c=2 is a valid uri
27:     url ||= "" # if original url was just '#', ''.split made url nil
28:     if args.any?
29:       url += "?"
30:       url += args.map { |arg| arg.split("=") }.sort.map { |arg| arg.join("=") }.join("&")
31:     end
32: 
33:     # unify arxiv page and pdf based on their identifier https://arxiv.org/help/arxiv_identifier
34:     url = url.sub %r{^arxiv\.org/(?:abs|html|pdf)/(?<id>\d{4}\.\d{4,5}(?:v\d)?)(?:\.pdf)?}, 'arxiv.org/abs/\k<id>'
35: 
36:     # unify rfc-editor.org pages based on their URL structures:
37:     # https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9338.html
38:     # https://www.rfc-editor.org/info/rfc9338
39:     url = url.sub %r{rfc-editor\.org/(?:rfc|info)/rfc(\d+)[^/]*$}, 'rfc-editor.org/rfc/\1'
40: 
41:     url = url.sub %r{^m\.youtube\.com/}, "youtube.com/"
42:     url = url.sub %r{^youtu\.be/}, "youtube.com/watch?v="
43:     url = url.sub %r{^youtube\.com/.*v=(?<id>[A-Za-z0-9\-_]+).*}, 'youtube.com/watch?v=\k<id>'
44:     url.sub(
45:       %r{^youtube\.com/playlist\?.*list=(?<id>[A-Za-z0-9\-_]+).*},
46:       'youtube.com/playlist?list=\k<id>'
47:     )
48:   end
49: 
50:   def self.random_str(len)
51:     str = ""
52:     while str.length < len
53:       chr = OpenSSL::Random.random_bytes(1)
54:       ord = chr.unpack1("C")
55: 
56:       #          0            9              A            Z              a            z
57:       if ord.between?(48, 57) || ord.between?(65, 90) || ord.between?(97, 122)
58:         str += chr
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     str
63:   end
64: 
65:   def self.silence_stream(*streams)
66:     on_hold = streams.collect(&:dup)
67:     streams.each do |stream|
68:       stream.reopen(File::NULL)
69:       stream.sync = true
70:     end
71:     yield
72:   ensure
73:     streams.each_with_index do |stream, i|
74:       stream.reopen(on_hold[i])
75:     end
76:   end
77: end

280:       paginate @origin.stories.base(@user).order(id: :desc)
281:     end
282: 
283:     @title = @origin.identifier
284:     @above = {partial: "for_origin", locals: {origin: @origin, stories: @stories}}
285: 
286:     @rss_link = {
287:       title: "RSS 2.0 - For #{@origin.identifier}",
288:       href: origin_url(@origin, format: :rss)
289:     }
290: 
291:     respond_to do |format|
292:       format.html { render action: "index" }
293:       format.rss { render action: "stories", layout: false }
294:       format.json { render json: @stories }
295:     end
296:   end
297: 
298:   def top
299:     length = time_interval(params[:length])
300:     if length[:placeholder]
301:       respond_to do |format|
302:         format.html { redirect_to top_path(length: "1w") }
303:         format.rss { redirect_to top_path(length: "1w", format: :rss) }
304:       end
305:       return
306:     end
307: 
308:     @stories, @show_more = get_from_cache(top: true, length: length) {
309:       paginate Story.top(@user, length, filtered_tags.map(&:id))
310:     }
311: 
312:     @title = if length[:dur] > 1
313:       "Top Stories of the Past #{length[:dur]} #{length[:intv].pluralize(length[:dur])}"
314:     else
315:       "Top Stories of the Past #{length[:intv]}"
316:     end
317:     @above = "stories/subnav"
318:     @below = "home/top"
319: 
320:     @rss_link ||= {
321:       title: "RSS 2.0 - " + @title,
322:       href: "/top/#{length[:param]}/rss"
323:     }
324: 
325:     respond_to do |format|
326:       format.html { render action: "index" }
327:       format.rss { render action: "stories", layout: false }
328:     end
329:   end
330: 
331:   def upvoted
332:     @stories, @show_more = get_from_cache(upvoted: true, user: @user) {
333:       paginate @user.upvoted_stories.includes(:tags).order("votes.id DESC")
334:     }
335: 
336:     @title = "Upvoted Stories"
337:     @above = "saved/subnav"
338: 
339:     @rss_link = {
340:       title: "RSS 2.0 - Upvoted Stories",
341:       href: user_token_link(upvoted_stories_path(format: :rss))
342:     }
343: 
344:     respond_to do |format|
345:       format.html { render action: :index }
346:       format.rss {
347:         if @user && params[:token].present?
348:           @title += " - Private feed for #{@user.username}"
349:         end
350: 
351:         render action: "stories", layout: false
352:       }
353:       format.json { render json: @stories }
354:     end
355:   end
356: 
357:   private
358: 
359:   def page
360:     p = params[:page].to_i
361:     if p == 0
362:       p = 1
363:     elsif p < 0 || p > (2**32)
364:       raise ActionController::RoutingError.new("page out of bounds")
365:     end
366:     p
367:   end
368: 
369:   def paginate(scope)
370:     StoriesPaginator.new(scope, page, @user).get
371:   end
372: 
373:   def get_from_cache(opts = {}, &)
374:     # don't cache if there's a user because they can have hidden stories; visitors can filter tags by cookie
375:     if Rails.env.development? || @user || filtered_tags.any?
376:       yield
377:     else
378:       key = opts.merge(page: page).sort.map { |k, v| "#{k}=#{v.to_param}" }.join(" ")
379:       begin
380:         Rails.cache.fetch("stories #{key}", expires_in: 45, &)
381:       rescue Errno::ENOENT => e
382:         # Rails.logger.error "error fetching stories #{key}: #{e}"
383:         yield
384:       end
385:     end
386:   end
387: 
388:   def tags_with_description_for_rss(tags)
389:     tags.map { |tag| "#{tag.tag} (#{tag.description})" }.join(" ")
390:   end
391: end

1: class SendWebmentionJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   def endpoint_from_body(html)
5:     doc = Nokogiri::HTML(html)
6: 
7:     if !doc.css('[rel~="webmention"]').css("[href]").empty?
8:       doc.css('[rel~="webmention"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
9:     elsif !doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org/"]').css("[href]").empty?
10:       doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org/"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
11:     elsif !doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org"]').css("[href]").empty?
12:       doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
13:     end
14:   end
15: 
16:   def endpoint_from_headers(header)
17:     return unless header
18: 
19:     if (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel="[^"]*\s?webmention\s?[^"]*"/))
20:       matches[1]
21:     elsif (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel=webmention/))
22:       matches[1]
23:     elsif (matches = header.match(/rel="[^"]*\s?webmention\s?[^"]*"; <([^>]+)>/))
24:       matches[1]
25:     elsif (matches = header.match(/rel=webmention; <([^>]+)>/))
26:       matches[1]
27:     elsif (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel="http:\/\/webmention\.org\/?"/))
28:       matches[1]
29:     elsif (matches = header.match(/rel="http:\/\/webmention\.org\/?"; <([^>]+)>/))
30:       matches[1]
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   # Some pages could return a relative link as their webmention endpoint.
35:   # We need to translate this relative link to an absolute one.
36:   def uri_to_absolute(uri, req_uri)
37:     abs_uri = URI.parse(uri)
38:     if abs_uri.host
39:       # Already absolute.
40:       uri
41:     else
42:       abs_uri.host = req_uri.host
43:       abs_uri.scheme = req_uri.scheme
44:       abs_uri.port = req_uri.port
45:       abs_uri
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def send_webmention(source, target, endpoint)
50:     sp = Sponge.new
51:     sp.timeout = 10
52:     # Don't check SSL certificate here for backward compatibility, security risk
53:     # is minimal.
54:     sp.ssl_verify = false
55:     sp.fetch(endpoint.to_s, :post, {
56:       "source" => URI.encode_www_form_component(source),
57:       "target" => URI.encode_www_form_component(target)
58:     }, nil, {}, 3)
59:   end
60: 
61:   def perform(story)
62:     # Could have been deleted between creation and now
63:     return if story.is_gone?
64:     # Need a URL to send the webmention to
65:     return if story.url.blank?
66:     # Don't try to send webmentions in dev
67:     return if Rails.env.development?
68: 
69:     sp = Sponge.new
70:     sp.timeout = 10
71:     begin
72:       response = sp.fetch(URI::RFC2396_PARSER.escape(story.url), :get, {}, nil, {
73:         "User-agent" => "#{Rails.application.domain} webmention endpoint lookup"
74:       }, 3)
75:     rescue BadIPsError, NoIPsError, DNSError, Errno::ECONNREFUSED, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError, TooManyRedirects, Zlib::DataError
76:       # other people's DNS/hosting issues (usually transient); just drop the webmention on the floor
77:       return
78:     end
79:     return unless response
80: 
81:     wm_endpoint_raw = endpoint_from_headers(response["link"]) ||
82:       endpoint_from_body(response.body.to_s)
83:     return unless wm_endpoint_raw
84: 
85:     wm_endpoint = uri_to_absolute(wm_endpoint_raw, URI.parse(story.url))
86:     send_webmention(Routes.story_short_id_url(story), story.url, wm_endpoint)
87:   end
88: end

3: class CommentsController < ApplicationController
4:   COMMENTS_PER_PAGE = 20
5: 
6:   caches_page :index, :threads, if: CACHE_PAGE
7: 
8:   before_action :require_logged_in_user_or_400,
9:     only: [:create, :reply, :upvote, :flag, :unvote, :update]
10:   before_action :require_logged_in_user, only: [:upvoted]
11:   before_action :show_title_h1
12: 
13:   # for rss feeds, load the user's tag filters if a token is passed
14:   before_action :find_user_from_rss_token, only: [:index]
15: 
16:   def create
17:     if !(story = Story.where(short_id: params[:story_id]).first) ||
18:         story.is_gone?
19:       return render plain: "can't find story", status: 400
20:     end
21: 
22:     comment = story.comments.build
23:     comment.comment = params[:comment].to_s
24:     comment.user = @user
25:     comment.hat = @user.wearable_hats.find_by(short_id: params[:hat_id])
26: 
27:     if params[:parent_comment_short_id].present?
28:       # includes parent story_id to ensure this comment's story_id matches
29:       comment.parent_comment =
30:         Comment.find_by(story_id: story.id, short_id: params[:parent_comment_short_id])
31:       if !comment.parent_comment
32:         return render json: {error: "invalid parent comment", status: 400}
33:       end
34:     end
35: 
36:     # sometimes on slow connections people resubmit; silently accept it
37:     if (already = Comment.find_by(user: comment.user,
38:       story: comment.story,
39:       parent_comment_id: comment.parent_comment_id,
40:       comment: comment.comment))
41:       render_created_comment(already)
42:       return
43:     end
44: 
45:     if params[:preview].blank? && comment.breaks_speed_limit?
46:       return render partial: "commentbox", layout: false,
47:         content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
48:     end
49: 
50:     if comment.valid? && params[:preview].blank? && comment.save
51:       comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}
52:       comment.story.touch(:last_comment_at)
53:       # not using .touch because the :touch on the parent_comment association will already touch the
54:       # upated_at columns up the reply chain to the story once
55:       comment.parent_comment&.update_column(:last_reply_at, Time.current)
56:       NotifyCommentJob.perform_later(comment)
57:       render_created_comment(comment)
58:     else
59:       comment.score = 1
60:       comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}
61: 
62:       preview comment
63:     end
64:   end
65: 
66:   def show
67:     if !(comment = find_comment)
68:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 404
69:     end
70:     if !comment.is_editable_by_user?(@user)
71:       return redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(comment, true)
72:     end
73: 
74:     render partial: "comment",
75:       layout: false,
76:       content_type: "text/html",
77:       locals: {
78:         comment: comment,
79:         show_tree_lines: params[:show_tree_lines]
80:       }
81:   end
82: 
83:   def show_short_id
84:     if !(comment = find_comment)
85:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
86:     end
87: 
88:     render json: comment.as_json
89:   end
90: 
91:   def redirect_from_short_id
92:     if @user&.is_admin? && params[:id] =~ /\A\d+\z/
93:       redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(Comment.find(params[:id]), true)
94:     end
95: 
96:     url = Rails.cache.fetch("c_#{params[:id]}", expires_in: nil) do
97:       story = Story.joins("join stories as s2 on stories.id = coalesce(s2.merged_story_id, s2.id) join comments on comments.story_id = s2.id")
98:         .where(comments: {short_id: params[:id]})
99:         .select(:short_id, :title)
100:         .first
101: 
102:       render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400 and return unless story
103:       Routes.title_path story, anchor: "c_#{params[:id]}"
104:     end
105: 
106:     # having looked up the given id as a comment short_id, we know it's safe to interpolate
107:     redirect_to url
108:   end
109: 
110:   def edit
111:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_editable_by_user?(@user))
112:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
113:     end
114: 
115:     if request.xhr?
116:       render partial: "commentbox", layout: false,
117:         content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
118:     else
119:       render "_commentbox", locals: {comment: comment, parents: comment.parents, redirect_on_submit: true}
120:     end
121:   end
122: 
123:   def reply
124:     if !(parent_comment = find_comment)
125:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
126:     end
127: 
128:     story = parent_comment.story
129:     comment = story.comments.build
130:     comment.parent_comment = parent_comment
131:     comment.comment = params[:comment].to_s
132:     comment.user = @user
133: 
134:     if !parent_comment.depth_permits_reply?
135:       ModNote.tattle_on_max_depth_limit(@user, parent_comment)
136:       if request.xhr?
137:         render partial: "too_deep"
138:       else
139:         render "_too_deep"
140:       end
141:       return
142:     end
143: 
144:     if request.xhr?
145:       render partial: "commentbox", locals: {comment: comment, story: story}
146:     else
147:       parents = comment.parents.for_presentation
148:       parents = CommentVoteHydrator.new(parents, @user)
149:       render "_commentbox", locals: {
150:         comment: comment,
151:         story: story,
152:         parents: parents
153:       }
154:     end
155:   end
156: 
157:   def delete
158:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_deletable_by_user?(@user))
159:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
160:     end
161: 
162:     comment.delete_for_user(@user, params[:reason])
163: 
164:     render partial: "comment", layout: false,
165:       content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
166:   end
167: 
168:   def undelete
169:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_undeletable_by_user?(@user))
170:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
171:     end
172: 
173:     comment.undelete_for_user(@user)
174: 
175:     render partial: "comment", layout: false,
176:       content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
177:   end
178: 
179:   def disown
180:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_disownable_by_user?(@user))
181:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
182:     end
183: 
184:     InactiveUser.disown! comment
185: 
186:     if request.xhr?

3: class ReadRibbon < ApplicationRecord
4:   belongs_to :user

6: 
7:   validates :is_following, inclusion: {in: [true, false]}
8: 
9:   def is_unread? comment
10:     return false if !user || new_record?
11: 
12:     (comment.created_at > updated_at) && (comment.user_id != user.id)
13:   end
14: 
15:   # I mostly extracted this method so there's an easy seam for /s/fieikd
16:   # For perf the count needs to get pushed up to fetching the list of stories.
17:   def unread_count comments
18:     @unread_count ||= comments.count { |c| is_unread?(c) }
19:   end
20: 
21:   # don't add callbacks to this model; for performance the read tracking in
22:   # StoriesController uses .bump and RepliesController uses update_all, etc.
23: 
24:   def self.expire_old_ribbons!
25:     where("updated_at < ?", 1.year.ago).delete_all
26:   end
27: 
28:   def self.hide_replies_for(story_id, user_id)
29:     if (ribbon = find_by(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id))
30:       ribbon.is_following = false
31:       ribbon.save!
32:     end
33:   end
34: 
35:   def self.unhide_replies_for(story_id, user_id)
36:     if (ribbon = find_by(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id))
37:       ribbon.is_following = true
38:       ribbon.save!
39:     end
40:   end
41: 
42:   # save without callbacks, validation, or transaction
43:   def bump
44:     if new_record?
45:       save!
46:     else
47:       update_column(:updated_at, Time.now.utc)
48:     end
49:   end
50: end

3: class Domain < ApplicationRecord
4:   has_many :stories
5:   belongs_to :banned_by_user,
6:     class_name: "User",
7:     inverse_of: false,
8:     optional: true
9:   has_many :origins, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
10:   has_one :moderation, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
11: 
12:   include Token
13: 
14:   validates :banned_reason, length: {maximum: 200}
15:   validates :domain, presence: true, length: {maximum: 255}, uniqueness: {case_sensitive: false}
16:   validates :selector, length: {maximum: 255}
17:   validates :replacement, length: {maximum: 255}
18:   validates :stories_count, numericality: {only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0}, presence: true
19: 
20:   validate :valid_selector
21: 
22:   after_save :update_origins
23: 
24:   def valid_selector
25:     return if selector.nil?
26: 
27:     if selector.include? "\n"
28:       errors.add(:selector, "no newlines")
29:       return
30:     end
31: 
32:     selector_regexp
33:   rescue RegexpError => e
34:     errors.add(:selector, "is an invalid Regexp: #{e.message}")
35:   end
36: 
37:   def self./(domain)
38:     find_by! domain:
39:   end
40: 
41:   def selector=(s)
42:     s = s.strip
43:     s = "\\A#{s}" unless s.starts_with?("\\A")
44:     s = "#{s}\\z" unless s.ends_with?("\\z")
45:     super
46:   end
47: 
48:   def selector_regexp
49:     Regexp.new(selector, Regexp::IGNORECASE, timeout: 0.1)
50:   end
51: 
52:   def update_origins
53:     return unless saved_change_to_selector? || saved_change_to_replacement?
54: 
55:     stories.find_each do |story|
56:       story.update_column(:origin_id, story.domain.find_or_create_origin(story.url)&.id)
57:     end
58:   end
59: 
60:   def find_or_create_origin(url)
61:     return nil if selector.blank? || replacement.blank?
62:     valid?
63:     raise ArgumentError, "Domain not valid: #{errors.full_messages.join(", ")}" if errors.any?
64:     raise ArgumentError, "Can't create Origin until Domain is persisted" if new_record?
65: 
66:     # github.com/foo -> github.com/foo
67:     # github.com/foo/bar -> github.com/foo
68:     identifier = if url.match?(selector_regexp)
69:       url.sub(selector_regexp, replacement)
70:     else
71:       # if the URL isn't matched, the identifier is the bare domain (handles root + partial regexps)
72:       domain
73:     end.downcase
74: 
75:     # because of rails associations, `origins` is scoped to current domain object
76:     # create_or_find_by returns the new origin record
77:     origins.find_or_create_by(identifier: identifier)
78:   end
79: 
80:   def ban_by_user_for_reason!(banner, reason)
81:     self.banned_at = Time.current
82:     self.banned_by_user_id = banner.id
83:     self.banned_reason = reason
84:     save!
85: 
86:     m = Moderation.new
87:     m.moderator_user_id = banner.id
88:     m.domain = self
89:     m.action = "Banned"
90:     m.reason = reason
91:     m.save!
92:   end
93: 
94:   def unban_by_user_for_reason!(banner, reason)
95:     self.banned_at = nil
96:     self.banned_by_user_id = nil
97:     self.banned_reason = nil
98:     save!
99: 
100:     m = Moderation.new
101:     m.moderator_user_id = banner.id
102:     m.domain = self
103:     m.action = "Unbanned"
104:     m.reason = reason
105:     m.save!
106:   end
107: 
108:   def banned?
109:     banned_at?
110:   end
111: 
112:   def n_submitters
113:     stories.count("distinct user_id")
114:   end
115:

117:     domain
118:   end
119: end

6: # identifier github.com/foo. That Origin's domain is set to github.com.

1: class NotifyCommentJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   def perform(*comments)
5:     comments.each do |comment|
6:       deliver_comment_notifications(comment)
7:     end
8:   end
9: 
10:   def deliver_comment_notifications(comment)
11:     notified = deliver_reply_notifications(comment)
12:     deliver_mention_notifications(comment, notified)
13:   end
14: 
15:   def deliver_mention_notifications(comment, notified)
16:     mentions = comment.comment.scan(/\B[@~]([\w-]+)/).flatten.uniq
17:     # Remove username of author and anyone already notified about the reply.
18:     # If they have email_replies off, a reply that @mentions them will not generate a
19:     # mention email. email_replies trumps email_mentions to minimize unwanted emails.
20:     to_notify = mentions - [comment.user.username] - notified
21: 
22:     # every user gets a Notification, which may be filtered out from those views so that unhiding a
23:     # story reveals the notifications
24:     to_notify = User.active.where(username: to_notify)
25:     to_notify.find_each do |u|
26:       u.notifications.create(notifiable: comment)
27:     end
28: 
29:     # but there's no recalling an email or pushover, so sending those has to reflect story hiding
30:     not_hiding_users = to_notify.left_outer_joins(:hidings).where(hidden_stories: {id: nil})
31:     not_hiding_users.find_each do |u|
32:       if u.email_mentions?
33:         begin
34:           EmailReplyMailer.mention(comment, u).deliver_now
35:         rescue => e
36:           # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{u.email}: #{e}"
37:         end
38:       end
39: 
40:       if u.pushover_mentions?
41:         u.pushover!(
42:           title: "#{Rails.application.name} mention by " \
43:             "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}",
44:           message: comment.comment,
45:           url: Routes.comment_target_url(comment),
46:           url_title: "Reply to #{comment.user.username}"
47:         )
48:       end
49:     end
50:   end
51: 
52:   def users_following_thread(comment)
53:     users_following_thread = Set.new
54:     if comment.user.id != comment.story.user.id && comment.story.user_is_following
55:       users_following_thread << comment.story.user
56:     end
57: 
58:     if comment.parent_comment_id &&
59:         (u = comment.parent_comment.try(:user)) &&
60:         u.id != comment.user.id &&
61:         u.is_active?
62:       users_following_thread << u
63:     end
64: 
65:     users_following_thread
66:   end
67: 
68:   def deliver_reply_notifications(comment)
69:     notified = []
70: 
71:     to_notify = users_following_thread(comment)
72:     to_notify.each do |u|
73:       u.notifications.create(notifiable: comment)
74:       notified << u.username
75:     end
76: 
77:     hiding_users = HiddenStory.where(story: comment.story).pluck(:user_id)
78:     to_notify.each do |u|
79:       next if hiding_users.include? u.id
80: 
81:       if u.email_replies?
82:         begin
83:           EmailReplyMailer.reply(comment, u).deliver_now
84:         rescue => e
85:           # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{u.email}: #{e}"
86:         end
87:       end
88: 
89:       if u.pushover_replies?
90:         u.pushover!(
91:           title: "#{Rails.application.name} reply from " \
92:             "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}",
93:           message: comment.comment,
94:           url: Routes.comment_target_url(comment),
95:           url_title: "Reply to #{comment.user.username}"
96:         )
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     notified
101:   end
102: end

4:     # this brings in all of config/routes, eg. root_path or story_short_id_url
5:     include Rails.application.routes.url_helpers
6: 
7:     # class setting
8:

11:     end

13:     # routes, alphabetically
14:     def comment_target_path comment, title = false
15:       options = {anchor: "c_#{comment.short_id}"}
16:       story = comment.story.merged_into_story || comment.story
17:       options[:title] = story.title_as_slug if title
18:       story_short_id_path(story, options)
19:     end
20: 
21:     def comment_target_url comment, title = false
22:       options = {anchor: "c_#{comment.short_id}"}
23:       story = comment.story.merged_into_story || comment.story
24:       options[:title] = story.title_as_slug if title
25:       story_short_id_url(story, options)
26:     end
27: 
28:     def title_path story, anchor: nil
29:       story_short_id_path(story, title: story.title_as_slug, anchor:)
30:     end
31: 
32:     def title_url story, anchor: nil
33:       story_short_id_url(story, title: story.title_as_slug, anchor:)
34:     end
35: 
36:     def url_or_comments_path story, *options
37:       story.url.presence || title_path(story, *options)
38:     end
39: 
40:     def url_or_comments_url story, *options
41:       story.url.presence || title_url(story, *options)
42:     end
43:   end
44: end

3: class StoriesPaginator
4:   attr_accessor :per_page
5: 
6:   STORIES_PER_PAGE = 25
7: 
8:   def initialize(scope, page = 1, user = nil)
9:     @scope = scope
10:     @page = page
11:     @user = user
12:     @per_page = STORIES_PER_PAGE
13:   end
14: 
15:   def get
16:     with_pagination_info @scope.limit(per_page + 1)
17:       .offset((@page - 1) * per_page)
18:       .for_presentation
19:   end
20: 
21:   private
22: 
23:   def with_pagination_info(scope)
24:     scope = scope.to_a
25:     show_more = scope.count > per_page
26:     scope.pop if show_more
27: 
28:     [cache_votes(scope), show_more]
29:   end
30: 
31:   def cache_votes(scope)
32:     if @user
33:       votes = Vote.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(@user.id, scope.map(&:id))
34: 
35:       hs = HiddenStory.where(user_id: @user.id, story_id: scope.map(&:id)).map(&:story_id)
36:       ss = SavedStory.where(user_id: @user.id, story_id: scope.map(&:id)).map(&:story_id)
37: 
38:       scope.each do |s|
39:         s.current_vote = votes[s.id]
40:         if hs.include?(s.id)
41:           s.is_hidden_by_cur_user = true
42:         end
43:         if ss.include?(s.id)
44:           s.is_saved_by_cur_user = true
45:         end
46:       end
47:     end
48:     scope
49:   end
50: end

3: class ApplicationController < ActionController::Base
4:   include IntervalHelper
5:   include Authenticatable
6:   include ApplicationHelper
7: 
8:   protect_from_forgery
9:   prepend_before_action :prepare_exception_notifier # first in case others raise
10:   before_action :heinous_inline_partials, if: -> { Rails.env.development? }
11:   before_action :mini_profiler
12:   before_action :set_traffic_style
13:   before_action :remove_unknown_cookies
14:   after_action :clear_session_cookie
15: 
16:   SESSION_DEFAULT_KEYS = %w[session_id _csrf_token]
17:   # match this in caddy config for bypassing the file cache
18:   TAG_FILTER_COOKIE = :tag_filters
19:   CACHE_PAGE = proc { @user.blank? && cookies[TAG_FILTER_COOKIE].blank? }
20: 
21:   # copied from https://github.com/rack/rack/blob/main/lib/rack/utils.rb
22:   VALID_COOKIE_KEY = /\A[!#$%&'*+\-.\^_`|~0-9a-zA-Z]+\z/
23: 
24:   # Rails misdesign: if the /recent route doesn't support .rss, Rails calls it anyways and then
25:   # raises MissingTemplate when it's not handled, as if the app did something wrong (a prod 500!).
26:   unless Rails.env.development?
27:     rescue_from ActionController::UnknownFormat, ActionView::MissingTemplate do
28:       request.format = :html # required, despite format.any
29:       respond_to do |format|
30:         format.any { render "about/404", status: :not_found, content_type: "text/html" }
31:       end
32:     end
33:   end
34:   rescue_from ActionController::UnpermittedParameters do
35:     respond_to do |format|
36:       format.html { render plain: "400 Unpermitted query or form parameter", status: :bad_request }
37:       format.json { render json: {error: "400 Unpermitted query or form parameter"}, status: :bad_request }
38:     end
39:   end
40:   rescue_from ActionController::ParameterMissing do |exception|
41:     respond_to do |format|
42:       format.html { render plain: "400 #{exception.message}", status: :bad_request }
43:       format.json { render json: {error: exception.message.to_s}, status: :bad_request }
44:     end
45:   end
46:   rescue_from ActionDispatch::Http::MimeNegotiation::InvalidType do
47:     render plain: "fix the mime type in your HTTP_ACCEPT header",
48:       status: :bad_request, content_type: "text/plain"
49:   end
50:   rescue_from ActionDispatch::RemoteIp::IpSpoofAttackError do
51:     render plain: "You have some kind of weird, implausible VPN setup. If you are not doing something naughty, please contact the admin to start debugging.",
52:       status: :bad_request, content_type: "text/plain"
53:   end
54:   rescue_from ActiveRecord::ConnectionNotEstablished, Trilogy::SyscallError::ECONNREFUSED do
55:     render plain: "500 The database is not taking our calls.", status: 500, content_type: "text/plain"
56:   end
57: 
58:   def agent_is_spider?
59:     ua = request.env["HTTP_USER_AGENT"].to_s
60:     ua == "" || ua.match(/(Google|bing|Slack|Twitter)bot|Slurp|crawler|Feedly|FeedParser|RSS/)
61:   end
62: 
63:   def check_for_read_only_mode
64:     if Rails.application.read_only?
65:       flash.now[:error] = "Site is currently in read-only mode."
66:       return redirect_to "/"
67:     end
68: 
69:     true
70:   end
71: 
72:   def remove_unknown_cookies
73:     cookies.each do |key, _value|
74:       next if key == TAG_FILTER_COOKIE.to_s # don't clear tag filters cookie
75:       next if key == Rails.application.config.session_options[:key] # don't clear session cookie
76:       next if key == "__profilin" && (Rails.env.development? || @user&.is_moderator?) # don't clear Rack::MiniProfiler cookie
77:       next unless VALID_COOKIE_KEY.match?(key)
78:       cookies.delete(key)
79:     end
80:   end
81: 
82:   # clear Rails session cookie if not logged in so caddy uses the page cache
83:   # https://ryanfb.xyz/etc/2021/08/29/going_cookie-free_with_rails.html
84:   def clear_session_cookie
85:     if clear_session_cookie?
86:       key = Rails.application.config.session_options[:key] # "lobster_trap"
87:       cookies.delete(key)
88:       request.session_options[:skip] = true
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   def clear_session_cookie?
93:     # If the session has been loaded, it will contain some default keys and not
94:     # be `empty?`
95:     !@user && session.keys.all? { |k| SESSION_DEFAULT_KEYS.include?(k.to_s) }
96:   end
97: 
98:   def find_user_from_rss_token
99:     if !@user && params[:format] == "rss" && params[:token].to_s.present?
100:       @user = User.where(rss_token: params[:token].to_s).first
101:       Telebugs.user id: @user&.token, username: @user&.username, email: @user&.email, ip_address: request.remote_ip
102:     end
103:   end
104: 
105:   def heinous_inline_partials
106:     do_heinous_inline_partial_replacement
107:   end
108: 
109:   def mini_profiler
110:     if @user&.is_moderator?
111:       Rack::MiniProfiler.authorize_request
112:     end
113:   end
114: 
115:   def prepare_exception_notifier
116:     if Rails.application.config.exception_notifier
117:       exception_data = {}
118:       exception_data[:username] = @user.username unless @user.nil?
119:       request.env["exception_notifier.exception_data"] = exception_data
120:     end

todos.content: Orient in the codebase and identify the central submission model
Map the Story contract and shared pieces it builds on
Trace story creation through URL handling, markdown, and ranking
Audit every dependent of the Story contract by area
Assess blast radius of contract change
Produce change + verification map and final JSON summary

content.text:
## Step 1: Orientation

This is the [Lobsters](https://lobste.rs) Rails codebase (`README.md:1`). It is a link aggregator/social-news app using MariaDB/SQL. Code is organized as standard Rails: `app/models`, `app/controllers`, `app/views`, `app/jobs`, `app/mailboxes`, `extras/` for domain utilities, `lib/tasks/` for scripts, and `spec/` for RSpec.

The central submission model is `Story` at `app/models/story.rb:3`. Users post, comment on, vote on, tag, flag, save, hide, and search `Story` records. Shared pieces it sits on:
- `ApplicationRecord` (`app/models/application_record.rb:3`) – base model with an Arel `select_fix` scope.
- `Token` concern (`app/models/concerns/token.rb:1`) – provides TypeID-based `token` generation/validation.

## Step 2: Story contract

`Story` (`app/models/story.rb:3`) exposes:

**Associations other records hang off**:
- `belongs_to :user` (`story.rb:4`)
- `belongs_to :domain` / `:origin` with counter caches (`story.rb:5-6`)
- `belongs_to :merged_into_story`, `has_many :merged_stories` (`story.rb:7-17`)
- `has_many :taggings` (`story.rb:18`), `has_many :tags` (`story.rb:35`)
- `has_many :suggested_taggings`, `:suggested_titles`, `:suggested_tagging_times`, `:suggested_title_times` (`story.rb:21-31`)
- `has_many :comments` (`story.rb:32`)
- `has_many :votes` / `:voters` (`story.rb:36-41`)
- `has_many :hidings` (`story.rb:42`), `has_many :savings` (`story.rb:43`)
- `has_one :story_text` PK-shared (`story.rb:44`)
- `has_many :links` / `:incoming_links` (`story.rb:45-51`)
- `has_one :moderation` (`story.rb:52`)
- `has_many :read_ribbons`, `:saved_stories` (`story.rb:53-54`)

**Shared behavior**:
- `include Token` (`story.rb:206`) for `token` generation/validation.

**Callbacks**:
- `before_validation :assign_initial_attributes, on: :create` (`story.rb:259`)
- `before_save :log_moderation, :fix_bogus_chars` (`story.rb:260-261`)
- `after_create :mark_submitter, :record_initial_upvote` (`story.rb:262`)
- `after_save :bust_comment_redirect_cache, :recreate_links, :update_cached_columns, :update_story_text` (`story.rb:263`)

**Core public surface**:
- Scopes: `base`, `for_presentation`, `not_deleted`, `unmerged`, `hottest`, `recent`, `newest`, `active`, `saved`, `hidden`, `tagged`, `top`, `categories`, `to_mastodon` (`story.rb:61-189`)
- Methods: `url=`, `description=`, `title=`, `calculated_hotness`, `update_score_and_recalculate!`, `update_cached_columns`, `recreate_links`, `update_story_text`, `is_gone?`, `can_be_seen_by_user?`, `merged_comments`, `similar_stories`, `save_suggested_tags_for_user!`, `save_suggested_title_for_user!`, `as_json`.

What a teammate must know before changing the contract: many associations use `dependent: :restrict_with_exception` (`comments`, `suggested_taggings`, `suggested_titles`, `hidings`, `savings`, `story_text`, `incoming_links`, `moderation`, `read_ribbons`, `saved_stories`), so destroying a Story will raise unless those dependents are handled first. `taggings`, `votes`, and `links` use `dependent: :destroy`. `merged_stories` nullifies on delete. The `StoryText` relation is PK-shared (`foreign_key: :id`), and `update_story_text` syncs title/description to it after save.

## Step 3: Story creation and ranking

Order of creation:

1. **Controller entry**: `StoriesController#create` (`app/controllers/stories_controller.rb:21`) builds `Story.new(user: @user)`, calls `update_story_attributes` and `update_resubmit_comment_attributes`.
2. **URL handling**: `Story#url=` (`story.rb:1168`) strips tracking params, normalizes ports, then sets `normalized_url` via `Utils.normalize` (`extras/utils.rb:10`) and calls `set_domain_and_origin` (`story.rb:1157`) which creates/finds `Domain` and `Origin`.
3. **Validation**: custom validate block (`story.rb:265-294`) checks URL format, duplicate detection (`check_already_posted_recently?` `story.rb:326`), banned domain/origin, brigading, tags, etc.
4. **Markdown**: `Story#description=` (`story.rb:649`) generates `markeddown_description` via `generated_markeddown_description` (`story.rb:687`) → `Markdowner.to_html` (`extras/markdowner.rb:41`).
5. **Initial attributes**: `assign_initial_attributes` (`story.rb:543`) assigns `short_id`, default `score`, `hotness`, `last_edited_at`.
6. **Save callbacks fire** (`story.rb:263`):
   - `fix_bogus_chars` (`story.rb:673`)
   - `log_moderation` (`story.rb:795`)
   - after_create: `mark_submitter` (`story.rb:846`), `record_initial_upvote` (`story.rb:867`) → `Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because` (`app/models/vote.rb:127`)
   - after_save: `bust_comment_redirect_cache` (`story.rb:320`), `recreate_links` (`story.rb:1132`) → `Link.recreate_from_story!` (`app/models/link.rb:66`), `update_cached_columns` (`story.rb:1136`), `update_story_text` (`story.rb:1143`).
7. **Ranking**: `calculated_hotness` (`story.rb:550`) combines tag hotness modifiers, author bump, comment points, merged story scores, and time decay. `update_score_and_recalculate!` (`story.rb:693`) recalculates `score`, `flags`, and `hotness` from votes.

## Step 4: Dependents of the Story contract

Grouped by area, every dependent with file:line and usage:

**Content and rendering**
- `Comment` (`app/models/comment.rb:5`) – `belongs_to :story, touch: true`; comments are the discussion tree attached to a story.
- `Tagging` (`app/models/tagging.rb:3`) – `belongs_to :story`; joins Story ↔ Tag.
- `Tag` (`app/models/tag.rb:6`) – `has_many :stories, through: :taggings`.
- `SuggestedTagging` (`app/models/suggested_tagging.rb:3`) – `belongs_to :story`.
- `SuggestedTitle` (`app/models/suggested_title.rb:3`) – `belongs_to :story`.
- `StoryText` (`app/models/story_text.rb:6`) – PK-shared `belongs_to :story`; stores rendered/article text for search.
- `Link` (`app/models/link.rb:4-7`) – `from_story`/`to_story`; derives links from story URL/description.
- `HiddenStory` (`app/models/hidden_story.rb:5`) – `belongs_to :story`; per-user hide state, calls `story.update_score_and_recalculate!`.
- `SavedStory` (`app/models/saved_story.rb:5`) – `belongs_to :story`; per-user save state.
- `ReadRibbon` (`app/models/read_ribbon.rb:5`) – `belongs_to :story`; tracks read position.
- `Domain` (`app/models/domain.rb:4`) – `has_many :stories`; domain-level moderation/counter cache.
- `Origin` (`app/models/origin.rb:9`) – `has_many :stories`; origin-level grouping/counter cache.
- `StoriesPaginator` (`app/models/stories_paginator.rb:33`) – hydrates per-user hidden/saved vote state on story lists.
- `Routes` (`extras/routes.rb:16-37`) – builds URLs from `comment.story.merged_into_story || comment.story`.

**Search**
- `Search#perform_story_search!` (`app/models/search.rb:229`) – queries `Story.base(...).for_presentation`, joins `story_text`, `domain`, tags, etc.
- `Search#perform_comment_search!` (`app/models/search.rb:117`) – joins `:story` for domain/submitter/tag/title filters.

**Statistics**
- `Stats.fill_stories_graph_cache` (`app/models/stats.rb:30`) – counts stories over time.
- `Stats.fill_active_users_graph_cache` (`app/models/stats.rb:16`) – counts stories per active user.

**Voting and flagging**
- `Vote` (`app/models/vote.rb:5`) – `belongs_to :story`; story votes set `story_id` with `comment_id: nil`.
- `User#can_flag?` (`app/models/user.rb:312`) – checks story flaggability.

**Inbound email**
- `InboxMailbox#parent` (`app/mailboxes/inbox_mailbox.rb:34-49`) – parses `In-Reply-To: story.<short_id>...` to find a Story and posts a comment on it.

**Moderation**
- `Moderation` (`app/models/moderation.rb:15`) – `belongs_to :story`; logs moderator edits/deletes/merges.
- `Mod::StoriesController` (`app/controllers/mod/stories_controller.rb:1`) – moderator edits/deletes/undeletes stories.
- `Mod::FlaggedController#flagged_stories` (`app/controllers/mod/flagged_controller.rb:9`) – lists flagged stories.
- `Mod::MailsController#parse_references_and_recipients` (`app/controllers/mod/mails_controller.rb:56`) – references stories in mod mail.
- `ModNote` / `ModMail` – called from story validation paths for acculturation/banned-domain notes.

**Controllers / views consuming Story**
- `HomeController` (`app/controllers/home_controller.rb`) – all list actions (`index`, `newest`, `recent`, `active`, `saved`, `hidden`, `top`, `category`, `single_tag`, `multi_tag`, `for_domain`, `for_origin`, `newest_by_user`, `upvoted`) scope `Story`.
- `StoriesController` (`app/controllers/stories_controller.rb`) – CRUD, voting, hiding, saving, preview, URL fetch.
- `CommentsController` (`app/controllers/comments_controller.rb:16-64`) – creates comments on a story; checks `story.is_gone?`.
- `StoryImageController` (`app/controllers/story_image_controller.rb:4`) – serves generated story card image.
- `StoryUrlsController` (`app/controllers/story_urls_controller.rb`) – lists story URLs.
- Views: `app/views/home/index.html.erb`, `app/views/stories/_listdetail.html.erb`, `app/views/stories/_singledetail.html.erb`, `app/views/stories/show.html.erb`, `app/views/search/index.html.erb` all call `story.can_be_seen_by_user?`.

**Background jobs**
- `CreateStoryCardJob` (`app/jobs/create_story_card_job.rb:5`) – generates `StoryImage` from story URL.
- `SendWebmentionJob` (`app/jobs/send_webmention_job.rb:61`) – sends webmention; guards with `story.is_gone?`.
- `NotifyCommentJob` (`app/jobs/notify_comment_job.rb`) – sends reply/mention notifications using `comment.story`.

**External / utilities**
- `Markdowner.rerender_db_markdown!` (`extras/markdowner.rb:25`) – rerenders all story descriptions.
- `FakeDataGenerator` (`lib/tasks/fake_data.rake:45`) – generates fake stories.

## Step 5: Identity and visibility guards

- **Find by short_id**: `StoryFinder#find_story` (`app/controllers/concerns/story_finder.rb:5`) uses `Story.find_by(short_id: ...)`.
- **Gone guard**: `Story#is_gone?` (`story.rb:761`) returns true if `is_deleted?` or submitter is banned with negative score.
- **Visibility guard**: `Story#can_be_seen_by_user?` (`story.rb:582`) allows view unless `is_gone?`, except for moderators or the submitter.
- **Controller guards**: `StoriesController#show` (`stories_controller.rb:166`) checks `can_be_seen_by_user?`; `CommentsController#create` (`comments_controller.rb:17-18`) checks `story.is_gone?`; voting actions (`stories_controller.rb:243-288`) check `is_gone?`.
- **List-scope guards**: `Story.not_deleted(user)` (`story.rb:76`) filters out deleted stories unless viewer is moderator or submitter. `Story.base` (`story.rb:61`) includes `not_deleted` and `mod_preload?`. `Story.not_hidden_by` (`story.rb:151`) excludes stories hidden by the user.
- **Comment guard**: `Comment` validate block (`comment.rb:156`) checks `story.try(:accepting_comments?)` which uses `is_gone?` and age.
- **Inbound email**: `InboxMailbox#required_info` (`inbox_mailbox.rb:71`) bounces if `parent.nil?`; `parent` (`inbox_mailbox.rb:34-49`) only resolves `story.<short_id>` format.
- **Search**: `Search#perform_story_search!` uses `Story.base(@searcher)` which applies `not_deleted` and hidden filters.

## Step 6: Blast radius

Ranked by risk to a Story contract change (how it stores/tears down attached data):

**Highest risk**
- `Comment` (`comment.rb:5`) + `Comment#update_associated_caches` (`comment.rb:520`) → `story.update_cached_columns`. Any change to how stories cache comments or tear down comments breaks counts/hotness.
- `Vote` (`vote.rb:5`) + `Story#update_score_and_recalculate!` (`story.rb:693`). Score/flags are central to ranking and karma.
- `Link` (`link.rb:4`) + `Story#recreate_links` (`story.rb:1132`). Deletes/recreates links on URL/description changes.
- `StoryText` (`story_text.rb:6`) + `Story#update_story_text` (`story.rb:1143`). PK-shared; title/description sync must stay consistent.
- `Tagging` (`tagging.rb:3`) + tag scopes. Most list views filter/join on taggings.
- `HiddenStory`/`SavedStory` (`hidden_story.rb:5`, `saved_story.rb:5`) + `StoriesPaginator#cache_votes` (`stories_paginator.rb:31-46`) and `HiddenStory.hide_story_for_user` → score recalculation + ReadRibbon updates.

**High risk**
- `Search#perform_story_search!` (`search.rb:229`) and `perform_comment_search!` (`search.rb:117`) – depends on `Story.base`, `story_text` joins, and taggings. Changing story deletion/visibility or attached tables changes search results.
- `StoriesController#show` (`stories_controller.rb:139`) and list controllers (`home_controller.rb`) – merged stories, comment threads, mod preload all depend on association contracts.
- `Moderation` (`moderation.rb:15`) + `Story#log_moderation` (`story.rb:795`) – edit/delete logging relies on `changes` and `tag_changes`.

**Medium risk**
- `NotifyCommentJob` (`notify_comment_job.rb`) – relies on `comment.story` and hidden-story filtering.
- `SendWebmentionJob` (`send_webmention_job.rb:61`) – guards on `is_gone?` and `url.blank?`; URL changes affect webmentions.
- `CreateStoryCardJob` (`create_story_card_job.rb:5`) – triggered on create/URL change.
- `InboxMailbox` (`inbox_mailbox.rb:34`) – relies on short_id format and Story existence for email replies.
- `ReadRibbon` (`read_ribbon.rb:5`) – tied to story_id and hidden-state flips.

**Lower but still to verify**
- RSS/JSON views, Mastodon posting/deletion, `Routes` helpers, `as_json`, `similar_stories`, suggestions, domain/origin counters.
